| | **Here's a recap of what we've accomplished since January 1st:**
* We won the first competitive special election of 2023, flipping a Virginia Senate seat blue!
* **And on Tuesday we won THREE critical seats in the Pennsylvania House – blocking Republicans from taking control of the chamber!**

Here's the truth: Without a strong base of DLCC members, we wouldn’t have been in the position to achieve these wins. And the GOP is hungry for a win – they poured _six figures_ into January’s special election, and we're bracing for an even bigger wave of spending to come. **We have a pivotal special election in New Hampshire coming up in less than two weeks – so now, we need this grassroots team to come together to build on these wins and keep the GOP at bay. But it looks like your 2023 membership is still inactive – take a look:** | **2023 DLCC MEMBERSHIP SUPPORTER RECORD**

clean_body: In order to make meaningful progress on these critical issues, we absolutely must take back the House majority | = | Team – On Tuesday, President Biden delivered his State of the Union address – the first of the 118th Congress – in which he shared his priorities for the coming year. But in order to make meaningful progress on these critical issues, we absolutely must take back the House majority, and that starts with keeping this seat BLUE. = | = | = I look forward to working alongside my colleagues to make real progress on reducing inflation, combating climate change, and safeguarding reproductive rights – and I am so grateful to have a President in the White House who understands the urgency of the current moment. But with Republicans in the House majority, we’re being blocked from doing the work that really matters. = | = | = Thank you so much, Hillary * | | = Paid for by Scholten for Congress Hillary Scholten for Congress PO Box 6233 Grand Rapids, MI 49510 United States | = If you would like to receive fewer emails, . name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
subject: Twitter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
So much for bias against the right wing on Twitter.
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20230209-3874-header&t=1&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)
‘An abuse of public resources. An embarrassment.’
Alexandria didn’t hold back when she summed up yesterday’s Oversight Committee hearing.
Rather than investigate price gouging or other issues affecting everyday lives, Republicans held a hearing to accuse Twitter of ‘colluding’ with Democrats to suppress conservative views online.
[Since taking office, Alexandria has fought to shine a light on Republican nonsense and hold Congress to a higher standard. If you want to help us demand accountability and fight back against the GOP’s disinformation machine, can you please chip in today so we can expose the truth?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230209.fMLnf6&refcode=em20230209-3874-P_FND_EGR_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230209.fMLnf6&refcode=em20230209-3874-P_FND_EGR_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)**
The truth is that there is no evidence of bias against the right-wing on Twitter. In fact, Alexandria revealed that **Twitter changed their own content moderation policy in 2019 in order to allow a racist tweet from Trump to stay up.** 1
After Trump tweeted that Alexandria and the rest of the Squad should ‘go back to where they came from,’ a Twitter employee in charge of content moderation flagged that the tweet was an _explicit_ violation of the company’s policy. So what happened? Twitter changed the policy and the tweet stayed up.
And that’s not all. Alexandria also highlighted how another Twitter account spearheaded an online harassment campaign that falsely alleged hysterectomies were being performed on young transgender children at Boston Children’s Hospital. These claims incited a wave of right-wing threats of violence against the hospital and its staff, culminating in a bomb threat last August. But to date, Twitter has refused to suspend the account.
[Alexandria is going to continue to hold Republicans and Big Tech accountable. But we need your help. The more money we raise from emails like these, the more resources we have to combat disinformation. Can you chip in $15.91 (our average donation) – or anything you can – today?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230209.fMLnf6&refcode=em20230209-3874-P_FND_EGR_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)
In solidarity,
Team AOC
1 - [Business Insider](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/16823?t=23&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230209?refcode=em20230209-3874-P_FND_EGR_OPP&t=24&refcode2=3874_2526796_E2ZFMA&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230209-3874-P_FND_EGR_OPP&t=25&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=26&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=27&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=28&akid=3874%2E2526796%2EE2ZFMA)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

clean_body: Just released - | | | | | Friend, The Cook Political report has just released its predictions for the 2024 election, and your district is listed front and center. Last year, this report listed NJ-07 as a “tilting republican” race. In 2024? Not only that, but your district is the only onelisted as being in competitive territory. This report can only mean one thing: Tom Kean’s re-election campaign will be an even tougher race than last time. We need to get ahead of this, friend, so we can start tilting the scale and moving the rating back to our side. Failing to get ready for this race TODAY means that New Jersey could be losing one of only THREE Republicans in its Congressional delegation. Help Tom get ready to fight: New Jersey is counting on you. — NJ GOP Alerts | | | Paid for by Kean for Congress Inc Copyright © 2023 Kean for Congress Our mailing address is: Kean for Congress Inc PO Box 999 Edison, NJ 08818 Click to mail your donation. * Working Together (Below)

Working Together Like many in Michigan's First District, I worry about my country. Can we work together to solve our problems? When half of the leadership offered ovations and others jeered, our national divisions were on full display during President Biden's SOTU address.
Is this what America needs? We need problem-solving, regardless of the source. We need leadership for all. We need unity, not divisive rhetoric. We must work together to address the needs of the 1st District, Michigan, and the Nation. It isn't a Republican-led House and Democratic-led Senate and President. It's the People's House, Senate, and President. The same goes for our State, county, and city governments. Throughout it all, we can never forget ordinary everyday people — the rural worker, our seniors, and our youth. We need to invest in people, protect our environment, and celebrate each other's individuality. We must embrace America's multiculturalism and our distinct vantage points. We must remember the role of government at all levels — local, state, and federal — is to serve, protect, and provide for the general well-being of its citizens. The people of Michigan's 1st District embody resiliency. Politically, we are as diverse as anywhere in the United States, but our commonalities allow us to show the Nation how united we can be. In Northern Michigan and the UP, we work hard and play hard, and we're all intricately linked with the hardships and successes of each other. We tackle problems with grit, strength, and loyalty to our friends, family, neighbors, and communities. If our representatives legislated with the same fiercely independent spirit as their constituents, the strength of our district would rival anywhere in the country.

name: Don Huffines
subject: HLF Roundup: 02/09/23
body:
The Huffines Liberty Foundation has been off to a tremendous start this year. So much so, that I think it’s time to launch a bi-weekly roundup of all the great work we are accomplishing.
No images? [Click here](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-e-firnx-yuddtktydi-f/) [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-r/) **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE** Press Manager
Contact: [Press@HuffinesLiberty.com](mailto:Press@HuffinesLiberty.com)
## **HLF Bi-Weekly Roundup**
Hello Patriots,The Huffines Liberty Foundation has been off to a tremendous start this year. So much so, that I think it's time to launch a bi-weekly roundup of all the great work we are accomplishing.It's no secret that things are not going well under Joe Biden. And it's also clear that things can improve here in Texas. Because of this, my personal work and the work of the Foundation are devoted to offering a friendly critique of what's wrong--and more importantly, offering solutions to increase economic liberty and prosperity in Texas.**On that note, I would like to share a few things all liberty-lovers will enjoy:** 1\. First things first, we released our "Liberty Budget" for Texas. Sky-high property taxes are the biggest drag on our state's taxpayers and our state's economy. This proposal for the Texas Legislature would result in the largest tax cut in state history and put the biggest component of Texans' property taxes on the path to elimination in just five years.** Read the "Liberty Budget" [HERE](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-y/).**2\. Keeping on the tax front, I published an op-ed in the _Dallas Morning News_ , which you should find interesting. Property taxes are a big issue in Texas, and legislators have gone about it all wrong. The good news is that fixing the issue is not rocket science. ** Read my op-ed [HERE](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-j/).**3\. Finally, an issue close to my heart: eliminating Texas' school M&O tax. HLF has hand-delivered to legislators a comprehensive policy roadmap to accomplish this in just eight years. This would reduce the property tax burden on all Texans, not just homeowners.** Read the policy paper [HERE](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-t/).**** View my personal message to legislators [HERE](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-h/).**** BONUS: An interview with my friend Jenna Ellis [HERE](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-k/).**As always, remember, _if we lose Texas, we lose the free world_.For Texas,Don Huffines
President, Huffines Liberty Foundation [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-u/)[](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-o/)[](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-b/) [Donate](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-n/) | [](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-p/) | [ Share ](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-fb-firnx-yuddtktydi-z/) | [ Tweet ](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-tw-firnx-yuddtktydi-v/) | [ Forward ](https://huffineslibertyfoundation.forwardtomyfriend.com/t-yuddtktydi-17329976-firnx-l-e)
---|---|---
[www.HuffinesLiberty.com](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-l-firnx-yuddtktydi-x/)[info@HuffinesLiberty.com](mailto:info@huffinesliberty.com)
(214) 526-3000 The Huffines Liberty Foundation is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization. Contributions are tax-deductible.
By completing forms on this website, you agree to receive marketing and communications from the Huffines Liberty Foundation. [Preferences](https://huffineslibertyfoundation.updatemyprofile.com/t-firnx-17329976-yuddtktydi-s) | [Unsubscribe](https://news.huffinesliberty.com/t/t-u-firnx-yuddtktydi-g/)
---

name: Jake Duesenberg
subject: Here's What We're Doing About It
body:
| [ ](http://www.action4liberty.com/?e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=1)
---
Peter \-- You spoke and we listened. **This Radical, Globalist, left-wing agenda on full display in St Paul is going to fundamentally change the direction of our state.** Democrats have already passed the most extreme abortion bill in the nation, allowing babies at 7 lbs and 39 weeks old to be murdered in the womb. And the Radicals at the Capitol also just passed a bill that will increase your energy costs, while making our energy system less reliable with a government mandate to be 80% off of fossil fuels in Minnesota in just 7 years; 100% off them by 2040! Meanwhile, there's a bill moving through the MN House that would make Minnesota a Sanctuary State for "Gender Affirming" surgeries - or what we know them as - abusive, child genital mutilation surgeries. We covered this on today's show: [](https://www.action4liberty.com/r?u=w5XKTZ0h8dEcNEoc_HghfKASHwnIic4yRIHUiEbNVfezHbX2FeFF3AXpJfUjL4hGVD6wb2kuwnNmOfRmxCZxjWjifpMuCppkbmNw4i4i_qlNdEz5rtYTgnx9nz_jyXmRPxxq2iShyTH4UoTxUAfvNQ&e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=2) It gets worse... **The Radicals are also moving these items through the legislature at record speed:**
* **Gun Control**
* **Tax Increases**
* **License Tab Increases**
* **Drivers Licenses for Illegals**
* **Dismantling Voting Security Protections**

The global, leftist agenda is a serious threat to us all. And now the frontlines of the implementation of that agenda is right here in Minnesota. I've heard other groups say "well, elections have consequences" then do NOTHING about it. Well I'm not fine with that. And neither are you.
## I've heard you loud and clear that YOU WANT ACTION.
Our name is Action 4 Liberty for a reason. When politicians vote against our liberties, we create political pain. Which is exactly what we are going to do. Because WE CANNOT LET the Radical Democrats kill our state without a fight. So I just authorized a MASSIVE field operation program which launched today in St Cloud and will likely span from the Iron Range to the North Dakota border down to Mankato. We're going to make things very painful for vulnerable Democrat legislators who are voting for this Globalist, Liberal agenda. We have a door knocking team on the ground right now in St Cloud letting Sen Aric Putnam and Rep Dan Wolgamott's voters know how they are voting for an EXTREME agenda. **Our field team took this stack of lit off my desk this morning and headed up north to get it on doors.** [](https://www.action4liberty.com/donate?e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=3) This is just the start. But I can't do it alone. Only with YOUR help can these operations be deployed. **In 2021, your contributions allowed us to:** **> >> Knocked on 40,000 doors** **> >> Made 55,000 targeted phone calls** **> >> Run digital ads with over a million impressions** All this culminated with ENDING WALZ' TYRANICAL EMERGENCY POWERS. Every day, as the battles get more intense, we want to be at the doors of independent voters in these battleground districts, educating them on how their Democrat is about to vote on more radical bills. Politicians care about one thing: getting re-elected. So the place to hit them where it hurts is by uncovering their radical agenda to their voters. But field operations cost A LOT of money. And they will tie up my staff full time. Which is something we are ready to do if you have our back. I desperately need to start hiring more field operatives IMMEDIATELY and get them into this battle. We have a top notch recruitment pipeline of young liberty professionals - all we need are the resources to get this going. **Please help me with a donation right now to help us grow this operation exponentially!!!** | [](https://www.action4liberty.com/donate?e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=4 "Donate") [Donate](https://www.action4liberty.com/donate?e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=4)
---
Listen, these pressure campaigns work!
In 2021, we picked 7 vulnerable Democrat targets so we could get a vote to End Walz' Emergency Powers.
* **3 of the 7 targets QUIT and didn't run for re-election in 2022.**
* **Another 2 of the targets (Reps Sandstede and Ecklund) got beat in the election.**
* **And the remaining two vulnerable Democrats are voting with Republicans against some of this Radical agenda already this year. They are TERRIFIED of what's to come if they get on board.**

This PRESSURE works! It's the only way to win this fight.
Which is why we are wasting no time. One of my field operatives, MAGA Jesse, just sent me these pictures from the lit drop in St Cloud.
[](https://www.action4liberty.com/donate?e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=5)
There are another three vulnerable Democrat Senators on our target list and six more Democrat Representatives.
It's vitally important that we reach their voters at the doors and let them know about this Radical Liberal agenda in St Paul.
Peter, can you help me build up these operations and put more doorknockers in these targeted districts?
We don't have much time. The Democrats are moving these radical bills at lightening speed. We MUST STOP THEM!
[**Please donate $25, $50, $100 or even $200. Or if you want to make a MASSIVE IMPACT, sponsor a door knocker for a week at $1,250 (fuel, lodging and pay).**](https://www.action4liberty.com/donate?e=44f3e77e3a436673570805f6f24387d1&utm_source=action4liberty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=here_s_what_we_re_doing&n=6)
And just like in 2021 when we forced the Democrat controlled House to End Walz Emergency Powers, we can fight back to stop their onslaught.
Thank you in advance!
**In Liberty,**
****
**Jake Duesenberg**
**President, Action 4 Liberty**

1920 Oakcrest Ave #5
Roseville, MN 55113

name: Team Sharice
subject: Sharice is taking a stand for Social Security and Medicare
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| During the State of the Union this week, President Biden and Democrats made it clear that we won’t permit any cuts to Social Security or Medicare as we work together to address the upcoming debt limit.

Hard-working Americans have paid into these important programs and millions of seniors and families rely on them every month.

While some Republicans have called for funding cuts for Social Security and Medicare, Sharice will always fight to protect Kansas families from these dangerous proposals — and needs to know if you’re with her.

**_[Add your name to our petition by midnight to show your support for Social Security, Medicare, and other social programs!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58516669/391360994/526495625?source=EM_NBI_SD_20230209_PT_1.2_0_3MO_campaign_NA_NA_socialsecurity.rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI0YTFjMS1lMGE4LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aMsB2syYU-BbKNwFlVjJJdkY4OSnaZZitAjWf--xf-U=&emci=4016cd67-dfa8-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=22b4a1c1-e0a8-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=22117458)_** | | [Sign On](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58516670/391360995/526495625?source=EM_NBI_SD_20230209_PT_1.2_0_3MO_campaign_NA_NA_socialsecurity.rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMmI0YTFjMS1lMGE4LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aMsB2syYU-BbKNwFlVjJJdkY4OSnaZZitAjWf--xf-U=&emci=4016cd67-dfa8-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=22b4a1c1-e0a8-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=22117458)

Sharice will work with anyone — Republicans, Democrats, and Independents alike — to ensure that these crucial programs are protected for every Kansan and every American.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20WAGOP?=
subject: 2023 Republican Action Conference
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
Come and join us for a day of learning and fun in Kennewick Washington on May 5th and 6th!

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .
Contact:
Steve Johnson
Communications Director
Washington State Republican Party
(425) 460-0570
steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org)

name: Kamala Harris
subject: As a dedicated grassroots supporter of the DNC, I knowyou’re committed to our work. Will you show your support after Joe’s speech by donating $7 to elect Democrats and continue fighting for a better America?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, **[www
body:
I hope you got a chance to tune in to the State of the Union address on Tuesday. I wanted to make sure you saw this note from President Biden about the work ahead. | |
---

clean_body: I hope you got a chance to tune in to the State of the Union address on Tuesday. I wanted to make sure you saw this note from President Biden about the work ahead. | | I hope you got the chance to watch Joe’s State of the Union address, Derek. It was so energizing to hear him speak about what we’ve accomplished and what’s next in our fight to build a better America. I know Joe already reached out, and I wanted to take a moment to uplift his message. Grateful to have you on this team, Kamala Kamala Harris Vice President of the United States \-----Forwarded message----- Sender: President Joseph R. Biden, Jr. Date: Tue, Feb 7, 2023 Subject: Let’s finish the job I just finished my State of the Union address. I hope I made you proud -- we’ve gotten a lot done together over the past two years. But there’s more to do to build an economy that works for working families. As I said tonight, we’ve got to finish the job. But here’s the deal: I can’t do it without your help, Derek. I’m grateful for everything you do, Joe Joseph R. Biden, Jr. President of the United States Manage how often you receive DNC emails, . If you no longer wish to receive emails from the DNC, **. If you're ready to elect Democrats in all 50 states, territories, and D.C., . Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible. Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, ** , and not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Supervisor=20David=20Canepa?=
subject: February 2023 e-newsletter
body:
| | | |
---
| | |
# 2023 February e-newsletter
News and information for the District 5 in San Mateo County.
---
|
---
|
---
|
#### **Time to heal following mass shooting in Half Moon Bay**
---
| Dear friends, First of all, the outpouring of support and love within Half Moon Bay and from all over the world has been immense following the most tragic mass shooting in San Mateo County’s history two weeks ago. I understand there is a lot of grief in our community and that this is the time to heal. As we mourn together the loss of these 7 farmworkers in Half Moon Bay, the County is providing mental health counselors and other support to the community. Our Crisis Response Team (CRT) from Behavioral Health and Recovery Services has been meeting with families in the reunification center and other locations in Half Moon Bay. CRT staff are trained in crisis response, suicide prevention, and other mental health interventions. They provide individual and group psychological first aid, grief support counseling, connection to needed ongoing services and crisis intervention counseling free of charge to residents in need.
---
The county is also working with the City of Half Moon Bay and local agencies to provide temporary housing, meals and other services to those directly impacted by the shootings. Keep in mind there are dozens of farmworkers out of work now because of this crisis and we are doing all we can to support them and I assure you the county is prepared to do all it can to help our community heal. The victims are identified as Zhishen Liu, 73, of San Francisco; Marciano Martinez Jimenez, 50, of Moss Beach; Qizhong Cheng, 66, of Half Moon Bay; Aixiang Zhang, 74, of San Francisco; Jingzhi Lu, 64, of Half Moon Bay; and Yetao Bing, 43 years and Jose Romero Perez. We honored the victims at our recent Board of Supervisors with a moment of silence and have made a pledge to improve the housing conditions for all farmworkers on the coast. Special thanks to Alas, the nonprofit on the coast, that has assisted farmworkers impacted by the crisis and for bringing the community together at several vigils over the past two weeks.
---
|
# **County proclaims January as Lunar New Year Month**
I was honored to present the county’s first-of-its-kind proclamation declaring January 2023 as Lunar New Year Month to Sophia Tseng, Board President, and Yung-Hui Chien, former Board President and long-time volunteer, of the North Peninsula Mandarin School in the city of San Mateo. Before I presented the proclamation, I acknowledged the tragedies that have impacted the Asian American community, our county and California as a state. From Monterey Park to our own community in Half Moon Bay, this Lunar New Year has been marked by significant pain and grief. Although it’s been a somber start to the New Year, we cannot let these tragedies mar that this is a time for renewal. Lunar New Year is a celebration of the arrival of spring and the beginning of a new year. Having started on January 22, our Asian American and Asian Pacific Islander communities are observing the Year of the Rabbit, considered to represent calm, positivity, and contemplation. As our communities observe this new year, may it bring healing and light to you and your families during this time.
---
---
| **[](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=9306d3d37b&e=c0babbc643)**
# **Please join me for Coffee with Canepa**

Please join me and some special guest speakers for our Caffeinate and Conversate with Canepa monthly events on Saturday, February 18 at Philz Coffee in Westborough Square in South San Francisco from 10 a.m. to noon.

This month’s topic is about mental health, a timely issue considering the mass shooting in Half Moon Bay and for those residents who were severely impacted by the devastating storms that caused significant damage from flooding, mudslides, power outages and downed trees.

We will hear about the resources the county provides related to mental health, including behavioral health counseling and learn about nonprofit advocates that help to improve mental health services throughout our county, reduce the stigma associated with mental illness and support effective treatment and recovery programs.

[RSVP NOW!](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=6823edd3bf&e=c0babbc643)
---
---
|
---
| **Recovering from the January storms**
---
| The heavy rains at the beginning of the year prompted San Mateo County to declare a State of Emergency Jan. 4 which qualifies the county to access FEMA funds for our recovery efforts. Now county residents impacted by the storms can also apply to FEMA for damages caused to their properties.

Starting Thursday Feb. 9, the county will standup a Disaster Recovery Center in San Mateo to help residents affected by recent storms learn about available assistance from local, state and federal agencies.

The center will operate daily from Feb. 9 through Wednesday, Feb. 22 (the center could close earlier if all residents have been served), at the San Mateo County Event Center, west parking entrance, 2495 South Delaware St., San Mateo, 94403 (Google map link)

Hours are 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. – including the weekend – in Cypress Hall.

Representatives from the county, state Office of Emergency Services and Federal Emergency Management Agency will provide a one-stop source for information with services that include employment assistance, record replacement, veterans’ assistance, referrals to agencies that may offer other assistance and information on Small Business Administration programs
---
If you sustained damages or losses due to the New Year’s storms, you can apply now for FEMA disaster assistance.

Disaster assistance may include grants to help pay for temporary housing and essential home repairs as well as other serious disaster-related needs such as medical and dental expenses, transportation, childcare and moving and storage expenses. The deadline to apply is March 16, 2023.

If you have insurance, first file a claim with your insurance provider. FEMA provides assistance to applicants for your uninsured or underinsured disaster-caused expenses and serious needs.

To apply visit [DisasterAssistance.gov](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=2b9aad0bea&e=c0babbc643) or call all the FEMA Helpline at 800-621-3362. Help is available in many languages.

Assistance from FEMA became available when San Mateo County was added to the “major disaster declaration” for the series of storms that pummeled the state beginning at the end of December 2022. Please share this information with anyone you think may qualify for the assistance.
---
|
---
|
# **Caltrain electrification now fully funded**
---
| As a member of the Metropolitan Transportation Commission and SamTrans Board of Directors, I’m pleased to announce Caltrain will receive $367 million in state funding to finish its electrification project.

This will be the most transformative public transportation project the San Francisco Peninsula will see in our lifetimes. This will take cars off the roads with increased service, reduce our carbon footprint and connect San Francisco and Silicon Valley to the rest of the Bay Area with virtually a seamless transportation network. Electrification will also pave the way for the future of high-speed rail which one day will connect most of California. It’s an exciting day. Go Caltrain. Choo choo!
---
Caltrain Electrification will electrify the corridor from San Francisco Caltrain Station to the Tamien Caltrain Station. Electrification improvements include converting diesel-hauled trains to electric trains, increasing service to six trains per peak hour per direction, and maintaining operating speed up to 79 mph.

The primary purpose of Caltrain Electrification is to improve Caltrain system performance and curtail long-term environmental impacts by reducing noise, improving regional air quality, and lowering greenhouse gas emissions.

Read the release from Caltrain here: [https://www.caltrain.com/news/caltrain-receives-367-million-state-funding-finish-electrification-project](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=a64033128f&e=c0babbc643)
---
|
---
|
# **MTC funds several local pedestrian and bicycle improvement projects**
---
| The Metropolitan Transportation Commission Board of Directors also approved a $300 million One Bay Area Grant Program last month, including a $34 million investment in San Mateo County to support several pedestrian and bicycle improvements from Colma, South San Francisco, Burlingame, Redwood City and Menlo Park.

Protecting children who walk or ride their bikes to schools or older persons trying to cross our busy traffic corridors just got a big boost. Some of these projects have been in the planning stages for years, including the El Camino Real Bicycle and Pedestrian Improvement Plan which kicked off in Colma back in 2019.

This shows a continued commitment by the MTC to use our federal dollars to improve safety for residents throughout the entire Bay Area. I proud to serve on the MTC to bring these much need federal and state dollars to the Peninsula.

---
The San Mateo County projects include:

Rollins Road Bicycle and Pedestrian Improvement in Burlingame for $3,100,000; and

El Camino Real Complete Street, Mission Road to SSF and Colma for $4,640,000
With an additional grant from C/CAG for $2,120,000; and

Middle Ave. Caltrain Pedestrian and Bicycle Undercrossing in Menlo Park for $5,000,000; and

Roosevelt Avenue Traffic Calming Project in Redwood City for $3,400,000; and

Bay Road Complete Street Rehabilitation in San Mateo County for $3,807,000; and

19th Ave/Fashion Island Blvd Complete Street Class IV SMC Transit Authority for $3,375,000; and

School St/Spruce Ave and Hillside Blvd Safety and Access Imps in South San Francisco for $3,128,000.
---
| **Battling food insecurity **
Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: **CalFresh:**
Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: [https://www.cdss.ca.gov/food-nutrition/calfresh](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=a7580a8c8e&e=c0babbc643) Contact: (650) 802-6576, [antorres@smcgov.org](mailto:antorres@smcgov.org) **Daly City Partnership:**
DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: [https://www.dcpartnership.org/covid19/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=cce7626597&e=c0babbc643) **Second Harvest Food Bank:**
Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: [https://www.shfb.org/get-food/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=48e6619031&e=c0babbc643) Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 **Interns wanted to join our team **

We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9. You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at mrichardson@smcgov.org

**Follow the Office on Social Media**
If you want to get more insight into our office please connect with us on social media. It’s also a great way to interact with the office and to get notifications on upcoming events. Click the icons below and like us on Facebook and follow us on Twitter.** ** In friendship, David J. Canepa
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | |
---
| _Copyright © 2023 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Supervisor David Canepa1618 Sullivan497Daly City, CA 94015
[Add us to your address book](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/vcard?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96&e=c0babbc643&c=a13149fbf6) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96&e=c0babbc643&c=a13149fbf6).

[](http://www.mailchimp.com/email-referral/?utm_source=freemium_newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=referral_marketing&aid=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&afl=1)

subject: The State of the Union revealed more accurately by a comedian than by the President
body:
Listen now (13 min) | Biden claimed progress, while The Daily Show's guest host D.L. Hughes observed the outrageous reality that journalists decline to acknowledge
| |

| | | [Open in app](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/the-state-of-the-union-revealed-more?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store) or [online](https://substack..kPGBdJrvFtsHvamU2hdpyGw8j3mlRBaJZWe3ESfmLYo?)

Feb 10
---
[The State of the Union revealed more accurately by a comedian than by the President](https://substack..kPGBdJrvFtsHvamU2hdpyGw8j3mlRBaJZWe3ESfmLYo?)
Biden claimed progress, while The Daily Show's guest host D.L. Hughes observed the outrageous reality that journalists decline to acknowledge
| | [Listen to preview](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=101809066&utm_source=podcast-email&play_audio=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTAxODA5MDY2LCJpYXQiOjE2NzYwMDA3NzMsImV4cCI6MTY3ODU5Mjc3MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.GzHnkBgqt4jBi0DxMJMNLrCZDuzZxnS0xp7oZp-lMrc&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email#play)|

[](https://substack.com/redirect/047b2cdb-9fab-4d43-889f-97b26efd2c33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | Shahid Buttar| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=101809066&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTAxODA5MDY2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY3NjAwMDc3MywiZXhwIjoxNjc4NTkyNzczLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.DKdnjCnMFusan6JinDfQ7Ct2lQHGFX5EmIAi7f9Q0h4&utm_medium=email&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=101809066&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTAxODA5MDY2LCJpYXQiOjE2NzYwMDA3NzMsImV4cCI6MTY3ODU5Mjc3MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.GzHnkBgqt4jBi0DxMJMNLrCZDuzZxnS0xp7oZp-lMrc&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=101809066&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTAxODA5MDY2LCJpYXQiOjE2NzYwMDA3NzMsImV4cCI6MTY3ODU5Mjc3MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.GzHnkBgqt4jBi0DxMJMNLrCZDuzZxnS0xp7oZp-lMrc)
President Joe Biden's second [State of the Union address](https://substack.com/redirect/91a962cd-d2ca-44fa-ab1f-152c2eb4b9a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) offered predictable platitudes, backslapping by chummy Democrats, and idiotic responses by [maniacal](https://substack.com/redirect/4b36d81c-dd66-4ab1-8f17-16905faa7070?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Republicans. It also offered more of the same outright lies for which Joe Biden has [long](https://substack.com/redirect/d2dc6459-ab38-4fa8-bbbc-3e0c32b3ab82?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) been [renowned](https://substack.com/redirect/5405b00a-62d4-4154-b3fb-7d34588d0912?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
It is especially revealing when journalists critique comedians who speak the truth they prefer to ignore.
### Biden's speech ignored reality
Biden claimed this week, for instance, that he "ran for President to fundamentally change things," despite running in 2020 on the [promise](https://substack.com/redirect/d046e885-04f3-46ef-8b66-57144ffbad2c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "nothing would fundamentally change."
More broadly, his depiction of progress belies the stark reality in America today: children stand at risk of being randomly [murdered](https://substack.com/redirect/b0b74cd3-45c7-49d3-bd7a-923f59ca5080?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by irate or mentally ill classmates, while [Black](https://substack.com/redirect/94c55201-c591-469a-bc03-528528def382?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Latino](https://substack.com/redirect/07f0bb66-dde3-474e-b7dc-ef6bd7092faf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Americans face the same threats from police paid public dollars to supposedly guard public safety. Meanwhile, violent right-wing [vigilantes](https://substack.com/redirect/f8fcc37e-adb5-41ec-a6c3-cc115fe62445?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) enjoy fame and [impunity](https://substack.com/redirect/248f2ec8-0fa5-4e54-b11a-e370d6e628f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for documented murder.
Medical debt is [sweeping](https://substack.com/redirect/c2632b69-6d6f-46bc-950d-5dcdba791073?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the country at the same time that life expectancy has [declined](https://substack.com/redirect/5731fdcc-012f-46b6-b5a7-1d2286f34557?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), inflation is [eroding](https://substack.com/redirect/eb46e51d-90f9-427d-b531-27981934b225?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) lifelong savings as [wealth](https://substack.com/redirect/01bd483f-521b-40a5-802b-9bc1f9fc101b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) & [income](https://substack.com/redirect/f81a1c75-a589-4f58-9e9f-e5aa22b38882?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) stratification grow more severe, and economic precarity has grown so [punishing](https://substack.com/redirect/c9b82594-f4d9-4c59-9def-9e3bffbb8a86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Americans have widely [delayed](https://substack.com/redirect/ee805c94-9ded-49c4-876a-fa6b5891b062?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or foregone childbearing. Biden's speech conveniently ignored his recent [decision](https://substack.com/redirect/4ec3fca5-f693-4403-b469-b0c141d1cc6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to throw railway workers under the train by forcing them back to work despite ignored demands for sick days--which would have supported public health in the midst of a pandemic.
That is to say nothing of saber rattling in both [Eurasia](https://substack.com/redirect/9c4c6deb-9ae1-453f-9bd0-8ede3309bcf8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the [Pacific](https://substack.com/redirect/56bbc212-82ca-4e82-b920-d43018d074f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a mounting global climate [calamity](https://substack.com/redirect/255c352c-b4b2-425c-a202-35e80e31a6b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and a Congress that continues to [subsidize](https://substack.com/redirect/dea0d849-1bf7-4471-8311-05beacf47c6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) fossil fuel companies and allow the CIA to operate in secret without any public visibility into its [repeatedly](https://substack.com/redirect/8f5beb39-4bf1-45c9-985c-87162c7a996d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [criminal](https://substack.com/redirect/78fdd2a9-534b-4d7c-9861-b7b614a00d67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) operations or even its [budget](https://substack.com/redirect/f976377f-1452-4df0-8086-8404e90c8378?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

### MLK saw all this coming
In 1963, Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. wrote [a letter from a jail in Alabama](https://substack.com/redirect/f9e78248-8f8a-4ce0-b467-5b989fbc65e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that quickly became one of the [most crucial political texts](https://substack.com/redirect/8d5a7d11-0f22-45de-8754-240aee626ff1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in American political history. In it, he responded to white clergy critical of his organizing and agitation for civil rights, explaining that their preference for "order" over "justice," and their willingness to urge patience in the face of long denied demands for basic rights, represented a failing of not only solidarity with oppressed people, but also reason, and the principles that they claimed animated their faith.
Correcting the feel-good history taught in American schools is critical....Obama's presidency set back civil rights by more than a generation.
King would have been disappointed in the figures enabled by his legacy, including the two Black politicians who have reached the White House: President Barack Obama and Vice President Kamala Harris.
Each of them embodied "the...moderate" decried by Dr. King, despite racial backgrounds that would have landed them outside his original formulation.
Obama's record has been [exposed](https://substack.com/redirect/d186a7ab-22a6-406e-ace4-52f93eb1253f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the years since he left the White House, but was in plain sight to [those of us who paid more attention than the press](https://substack.com/redirect/c82e507e-654e-442d-b334-e4f347b591db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) when he was in office.
Beyond [siding with Wall Street](https://substack.com/redirect/58362d41-6886-4238-9e7e-fa7cf53c4866?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over working Americans, [escalating U.S. human rights abuses](https://substack.com/redirect/8e5e8091-d9c1-4066-bd90-d32586c6c2d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) abroad (after thankfully [curtailing](https://substack.com/redirect/c51e1c42-e8d2-4c1e-bb10-46c92c2efe18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) some of them only to then [give the criminals carte blanche](https://substack.com/redirect/990350b7-23e2-4f43-bfb0-eb9ef0d9a0e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)), entrenching the Bush-Cheney assault on constitutional civil liberties (which in turn [created the attack surface](https://substack.com/redirect/850a4b57-a6f4-44fc-9a71-e42a8c5793d6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for today's GOP majority in the House), and further entrenching the [longstanding racket](https://substack.com/redirect/4ad567ad-d9bc-4fb8-bd5c-c52efecb6a71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of private corporate health insurance, President Obama also [did more or less nothing](https://substack.com/redirect/45a8a5f2-76a9-4f0d-b175-3db466eb81fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to address the racism pervading policing, going so far as to [invite a white police officer](https://substack.com/redirect/529b5496-e904-42f5-9730-87a30a76cab6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who racially profiled a Black Harvard professor (who he knew and reportedly considered a friend) to the White House for a beer.
Despite professional obligations to rigor, transparency, and accountability, journalists spent the entire eight years of Obama's administration building a cult of personality that persists to this day.
Journalism, it seems, has learned nothing from its institutional failures.
Beyond the temporary recognition of voting rights and de-segregation principles that were also eventually turned on their heads, [the civil rights movement was mostly unsuccessful in achieving its policy goals](https://substack.com/redirect/b3513b92-ea7e-4509-84e2-5600c97e10d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Correcting the feel-good history taught in American schools is critical. Most Americans learn a version of history that ignores how contemporary institutions carry forward the legacy of institutional racism that defines the United States (and the distribution of capital within it) to this day. That's largely why we confront the specter of "white pride" in the U.S. today, as largely rural populations respond to scarcity by striking out at scapegoats.
But more specifically, judges and Justices of the Supreme Court have also falsely internalized a narrative of progress belied by the facts. In _[Shelby County v Holder](https://substack.com/redirect/c88e22c0-c199-406d-a262-272dadad291b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , Justices observing the election of a Black president decided that communities of color no longer needed presumptions favoring their right to participate in elections, even in jurisdictions with a long history of discrimination. By striking down the pre-clearance provisions of the Voting Rights Act in 2013, the Court effectively repealed its enforcement mechanism and rolled back the clock on civil rights.
In other words, Obama's presidency arguably set back civil rights by more than a generation, even as its [symbolism](https://substack.com/redirect/0c943792-0347-41d3-8eef-8695b42aa8d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) seemed to herald a paradigm shift.
What America got--instead of reforms to dismantle [the intersecting evils](https://substack.com/redirect/d20bed57-ad01-48df-8f91-3c01d759e6ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of capitalism, racism, and militarism decried by MLK--were racially diverse individuals in positions of influence who do the dirty work of white capital while wearing dark skin. They range from the Mayor of San Francisco to the former President of the United States, and from our city's former prosecutor who now serves as a Vice President to, sadly, many voices in Congress.
For now, let's leave aside the distribution of wealth and capital. I hope to examine that in a future article addressing issues including reparations for slavery, and the need for intergenerational entitlements commensurate with the four century period of forced labor. Paid subscribers can access below a draft introduction for that forthcoming post.
What Mr. Hughes & the Daily Show observed-via-insinuation may be horribly unfortunate, but it is also painfully true: Democrats have proven entirely useless on the issue of police reform, and Kamala Harris, in particular, went to the White House in 2020 in the midst of the largest grassroots uprising in American history, after building her career on the backs of communities and families composed of people who look like her.
Long before Biden went to the White House, he [crafted](https://substack.com/redirect/21b3ee09-b2b7-4b36-a65b-9a0dbd0aaf51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the bill that, in the 1990s, inflated our nation's prison population and swelled the ranks of [legal slaves](https://substack.com/redirect/071d5b88-4b52-42c3-abd1-3c7c9441d6ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As Chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, he also played a role in the [CIA running crack cocaine into U.S. cities](https://substack.com/redirect/f8be2be9-993d-4b99-9ea4-cbfa2c62ebc7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and flooding the prisons even further. Biden also infamously [put Clarence Thomas on the bench](https://substack.com/redirect/7d024509-2515-463a-bd01-4b887cffc214?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), enabling a continuing parade of horrors as the rule of law [crumbles](https://substack.com/redirect/35f62a58-6d51-4fed-ade6-772620fb38b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the face of politicized so-called "justice."
The complicity of Democrats in predatory policing and mass industrial slavery extends well beyond Biden and Harris. As Speaker of the House, Nancy Pelosi once [instructed](https://substack.com/redirect/7cfaf59f-f11b-42df-8839-f5a9ca0e31dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Democrats in Congress to ignore the pleas of activists seeking human rights in the face of relentless predatory state violence. That dovetails neatly with her historical [role](https://substack.com/redirect/95677a90-0cca-4067-bc2d-bdd27e86efae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) enabling CIA torture and potential liability under international law for human rights abuses, or her [inability](https://substack.com/redirect/86354dbe-b816-494e-955e-56cc9430b266?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to respond to immigrant rights activists incensed by her [critical role](https://substack.com/redirect/e84fbacd-b089-42ab-becf-b875b29c07dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) enabling human rights abuses at (and well beyond) our nation's borders.
While Dr. King included a specific racial descriptor when identifying the crucial impediment to justice, "the...moderate" remains in an only more entrenched position today than when he was alive.
### A record typical for predatory prosecutors
In the years since Kamala Harris served as San Francisco's District Attorney, a movement has crossed the country challenging the racially biased patterns & practices of prosecutors and police departments. Ironically, San Francisco [briefly emerged](https://substack.com/redirect/be903591-64c1-410d-98c3-350ed96aec19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as one of the country's most crucial locales embodying that movement, before the city's establishment [struck back](https://substack.com/redirect/63768ef6-b05a-4f98-9732-1f4c5150a698?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), restored the longstanding deference to police that characterizes every city across the United States, and then [placed a police propagandist](https://substack.com/redirect/b3f407f6-a7c8-49ed-b95e-051734bb44af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on our elected Board of Supervisors.
San Francisco enjoys a bizarrely progressive reputation, based largely on a counterculture that emerged in our city (with its epicenter at the intersection where I now live) 60 years ago.
Its longer history, however, reflects a sadly different pattern. San Francisco's racist history has no shortage of lowlights, including:
* [race riots](https://substack.com/redirect/d712aab6-fe6b-4844-aee4-9e843d6ef21f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg);
* [redlining](https://substack.com/redirect/979cb612-e9b5-436b-acf6-6e6469802694?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg);
* the [forced expulsion](https://substack.com/redirect/13d496ae-703f-4d83-ae6e-1531979c93ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Asians (long before the most recent [expulsion](https://substack.com/redirect/47b8a9a8-cb77-45e7-948e-87894667421b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Black residents and preceding [theft](https://substack.com/redirect/492e8777-c1b1-4873-b4e0-e16649c1fc7f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Black [wealth](https://substack.com/redirect/e8cc41ef-d77c-439a-8100-29eaf7813e49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg));
* ongoing [environmental](https://substack.com/redirect/fa96c5b1-cf18-46ee-8ede-a6cc781d7812?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) racism;
* racist [journalism](https://substack.com/redirect/27ff7ea8-5d53-4f4e-ab1b-d195cf49bf3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) protecting [corrupt oligarchs](https://substack.com/redirect/c82e507e-654e-442d-b334-e4f347b591db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); and
* absurdly severe racial disparities in wealth, [income](https://substack.com/redirect/d47dc1f0-c07a-4a7a-91b1-fc38f9ef6809?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [incarceration](https://substack.com/redirect/e221223c-e084-4922-90b7-c21c7dea7d5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as well as housing [assessments](https://substack.com/redirect/4ddf647c-bd9a-46d9-bd7d-3f1e09f0f789?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [employment](https://substack.com/redirect/946eeb8a-0f18-4866-b911-14400343cd6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) opportunities.

As a traditional, predatory prosecutor, Harris stood with our city's racist history, rather than calls for long [overdue](https://substack.com/redirect/37ad2aa0-2a62-4498-bb93-e816fdbe4c60?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (and ultimately still inadequate) police reform. The evidence is sadly voluminous.
[Holding Biden Accountable @WaitingOnBiden🧵 THREAD: Here are just a few of the many ways Kamala Harris has enabled and exacerbated police brutality during her career as "Top Cop." 1/11 ](https://substack.com/redirect/0f95a5f0-1c1f-42e1-85a7-9966c167f758?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[6:07 PM ∙ Jan 28, 2023
* * *
1,342Likes421Retweets](https://substack.com/redirect/0f95a5f0-1c1f-42e1-85a7-9966c167f758?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### Strap in
The 2024 presidential primary is already underway, with several candidates campaigning for the Republican nomination. Many Democrats, meanwhile, are questioning whether Biden should run again given his advancing age, which could make Harris the likely nominee despite her record in California and [equally disappointing job performance](https://substack.com/redirect/fcef1880-c4cf-464d-a4b2-9a533ef58cbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Washington.
I emphatically hope that Biden steps aside, and am eager to see a voice emerge from the Left to challenge him and/or Harris not only in the Democratic primary--but if necessary, as an Independent in the 2024 general election. Too much hangs in the balance to defer to a Democratic Party establishment whose [corruption](https://substack.com/redirect/7d02246c-78e8-4c1d-b24f-910916f52010?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has [repeatedly](https://substack.com/redirect/9d93c0b9-c6c4-4a96-9ccf-8947fb97c22b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [revealed](https://substack.com/redirect/4ec3fca5-f693-4403-b469-b0c141d1cc6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) itself.
Paid subscribers can access an introduction to an upcoming post exploring the issue of reparations, which has been in the news lately but predictably lacked any meaningful context...

Hughes observed the outrageous reality that journalists decline to acknowledge | | | ---|---|--- | | Shahid Buttar| | | | | | President Joe Biden's second offered predictable platitudes, backslapping by chummy Democrats, and idiotic responses by Republicans. It also offered more of the same outright lies for which Joe Biden has been . It is especially revealing when journalists critique comedians who speak the truth they prefer to ignore. Biden's speech ignored reality Biden claimed this week, for instance, that he "ran for President to fundamentally change things," despite running in 2020 on the that "nothing would fundamentally change." More broadly, his depiction of progress belies the stark reality in America today: children stand at risk of being randomly by irate or mentally ill classmates, while and Americans face the same threats from police paid public dollars to supposedly guard public safety. Meanwhile, violent right-wing enjoy fame and for documented murder. Medical debt is the country at the same time that life expectancy has , inflation is lifelong savings as & stratification grow more severe, and economic precarity has grown so that Americans have widely or foregone childbearing. Biden's speech conveniently ignored his recent to throw railway workers under the train by forcing them back to work despite ignored demands for sick days--which would have supported public health in the midst of a pandemic. That is to say nothing of saber rattling in both and the , a mounting global climate , and a Congress that continues to fossil fuel companies and allow the CIA to operate in secret without any public visibility into its operations or even its . I write this newsletter to expose the bipartisan corruption in Washington actively defended by press outlets. If you're learning from what you're reading, please subscribe to stay in the loop! Presidents manage to gaslight the public only due to the of the press, which has proudly embraced Biden's smug self-congratulation in defense of his administration. Ignorance reveals itself in many corners The police murder of Tyre Nichols has yet again sparked a national dialog that we to like to as soon as the press finds distractions from reporting on the state violence that continues to plague low-income communities of color across the United States. That dialog, in turn, has revealed the ignorance of the press establishment building a cult of personality around Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris. It is especially revealing when journalists critique comedians who speak the truth they prefer to ignore. Last Saturday, , whose former hosts include Trevor Noah and Jon Stewart. Hughes aired a clip critical of Vice President Kamala Harris, along with a monologue sharply critical of her record as a traditional (i.e. predatory) prosecutor in San Francisco before moving on to become California's Attorney General, then a U.S. Senator, and now, the Vice President of the United States. Most Americans never get to read or hear independent perspectives, because they rely on media outlets that ultimately serve the establishment of which they're a part. Help inform your friends! Ignorance reveals itself in many forms, including the self-righteous indignation of voices fairly described as "the white moderate" by Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. who chafe at the observations of justice seekers less beholden to the establishment that they serve. Today, many of those figures aren't even white. Democrats have proven entirely useless on the issue of police reform, and Kamala Harris, in particular, went to the White House in 2020 in the midst of the largest grassroots uprising in American history, after building her career on the backs of communities and families composed of people who look like her. Few figures demonstrate the prescience of Dr. King's analysis--and the charity of his formulation including a racial descriptor--than Vice President Harris. And few stories better demonstrate the role of mass media in enabling predatory state violence than expressing faux outrage at The Daily Show for calling attention to Harris' record as a career predatory prosecutor. One hopes journalists might spare some outrage for the complicit public officials they instead absurdly choose to lionize. MLK saw all this coming In 1963, Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. wrote that quickly became one of the in American political history. In it, he responded to white clergy critical of his organizing and agitation for civil rights, explaining that their preference for "order" over "justice," and their willingness to urge patience in the face of long denied demands for basic rights, represented a failing of not only solidarity with oppressed people, but also reason, and the principles that they claimed animated their faith. Correcting the feel-good history taught in American schools is critical....Obama's presidency set back civil rights by more than a generation. King would have been disappointed in the figures enabled by his legacy, including the two Black politicians who have reached the White House: President Barack Obama and Vice President Kamala Harris. Each of them embodied "the...moderate" decried by Dr. King, despite racial backgrounds that would have landed them outside his original formulation. Obama's record has been in the years since he left the White House, but was in plain sight to when he was in office. Beyond over working Americans, abroad (after thankfully some of them only to then ), entrenching the Bush-Cheney assault on constitutional civil liberties (which in turn for today's GOP majority in the House), and further entrenching the of private corporate health insurance, President Obama also to address the racism pervading policing, going so far as to who racially profiled a Black Harvard professor (who he knew and reportedly considered a friend) to the White House for a beer. Despite professional obligations to rigor, transparency, and accountability, journalists spent the entire eight years of Obama's administration building a cult of personality that persists to this day. Journalism, it seems, has learned nothing from its institutional failures. Beyond the temporary recognition of voting rights and de-segregation principles that were also eventually turned on their heads, . Correcting the feel-good history taught in American schools is critical. Most Americans learn a version of history that ignores how contemporary institutions carry forward the legacy of institutional racism that defines the United States (and the distribution of capital within it) to this day. That's largely why we confront the specter of "white pride" in the U.S. today, as largely rural populations respond to scarcity by striking out at scapegoats. But more specifically, judges and Justices of the Supreme Court have also falsely internalized a narrative of progress belied by the facts. In , Justices observing the election of a Black president decided that communities of color no longer needed presumptions favoring their right to participate in elections, even in jurisdictions with a long history of discrimination. By striking down the pre-clearance provisions of the Voting Rights Act in 2013, the Court effectively repealed its enforcement mechanism and rolled back the clock on civil rights. In other words, Obama's presidency arguably set back civil rights by more than a generation, even as its seemed to herald a paradigm shift. What America got--instead of reforms to dismantle of capitalism, racism, and militarism decried by MLK--were racially diverse individuals in positions of influence who do the dirty work of white capital while wearing dark skin. They range from the Mayor of San Francisco to the former President of the United States, and from our city's former prosecutor who now serves as a Vice President to, sadly, many voices in Congress. For now, let's leave aside the distribution of wealth and capital. I hope to examine that in a future article addressing issues including reparations for slavery, and the need for intergenerational entitlements commensurate with the four century period of forced labor. Paid subscribers can access below a draft introduction for that forthcoming post. What Mr. Hughes & the Daily Show observed-via-insinuation may be horribly unfortunate, but it is also painfully true: Democrats have proven entirely useless on the issue of police reform, and Kamala Harris, in particular, went to the White House in 2020 in the midst of the largest grassroots uprising in American history, after building her career on the backs of communities and families composed of people who look like her. As Chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, he also played a role in the and flooding the prisons even further. Biden also infamously , enabling a continuing parade of horrors as the rule of law in the face of politicized so-called "justice." The complicity of Democrats in predatory policing and mass industrial slavery extends well beyond Biden and Harris. As Speaker of the House, Nancy Pelosi once Democrats in Congress to ignore the pleas of activists seeking human rights in the face of relentless predatory state violence. That dovetails neatly with her historical enabling CIA torture and potential liability under international law for human rights abuses, or her to respond to immigrant rights activists incensed by her enabling human rights abuses at (and well beyond) our nation's borders. While Dr. King included a specific racial descriptor when identifying the crucial impediment to justice, "the...moderate" remains in an only more entrenched position today than when he was alive. A record typical for predatory prosecutors In the years since Kamala Harris served as San Francisco's District Attorney, a movement has crossed the country challenging the racially biased patterns & practices of prosecutors and police departments. Ironically, San Francisco as one of the country's most crucial locales embodying that movement, before the city's establishment , restored the longstanding deference to police that characterizes every city across the United States, and then on our elected Board of Supervisors. San Francisco enjoys a bizarrely progressive reputation, based largely on a counterculture that emerged in our city (with its epicenter at the intersection where I now live) 60 years ago. Its longer history, however, reflects a sadly different pattern. San Francisco's racist history has no shortage of lowlights, including: ; ; the of Asians (long before the most recent of Black residents and preceding of Black ); ongoing racism; racist protecting ; and absurdly severe racial disparities in wealth, , and , as well as housing and opportunities. name: Aaron Withe, Freedom Foundation
subject: Unions collected millions in COVID relief funds. We’re exposing them
body:
| | Please see a special message from our sponsor.
---
| | | | [](http://trk.libertyledger._c291cmNlX2NvZGU9TExPMzU2MDEifQ/ubn4RYCh1k7ndTQJdNY0EA)
---
Fellow American, While small business owners were struggling to keep their doors open in 2020, labor unions were busy profiting from the pandemic. Yes -- that's right, your tax dollars went into the hands of union bosses to the tune of MILLIONS. **_Freedom Foundation has exposed hundreds of unions around the country that submitted fraudulent applications and robbed American taxpayers of $36 million in Paycheck Protection Program loans._** I want to ensure that as many freedom-loving Americans as possible know the truth about these labor unions - truths that the liberal media and Big Tech want to keep under wraps! [**That's why I'm sharing our labor union exposé -_Profiting from a Pandemic: How Ineligible Unions Collected Millions in Federal COVID Relief Funds_ \- with you for FREE. Click here to download the report and see the details about this massive union fraud.**](http://trk.libertyledger._c291cmNlX2NvZGU9TExPMzU2MDEifQ/UsEcS-ZrODqfMbyAxUycOQ)
---
[ Labor Unions Exposed: Profiting from a Pandemic](http://trk.libertyledger._c291cmNlX2NvZGU9TExPMzU2MDEifQ/qoUCM8U3BOlW9vciWSqcQw)
---
As you probably remember, at the height of the pandemic, Congress created the Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) for small businesses and nonprofit organizations to cover payroll, rent, and utility payments to ensure they could pay their employees while being forced to close their doors. But labor unions - including teachers' unions - saw PPP as a cash cow, despite being ineligible to apply for the loans. **_For decades, corrupt union bosses have ripped off workers with mandatory "dues," while rigging the system in their favor, so it should come as no surprise that hundreds of unions had no problem committing mass fraud against the federal government._** At the end of the day, all the union bosses care about is lining their deep pockets to prop up the left's progressive machinery and push a radical, unconstitutional agenda. [**At Freedom Foundation, we're working to expose the truth about labor unions and the fraud they've committed. You can read more about this $36 million in fraud by clicking here.**](http://trk.libertyledger._c291cmNlX2NvZGU9TExPMzU2MDEifQ/amqP5k-_jagflC1ois9x4g) Fellow American, at least a dozen teachers' unions and advocacy groups received PPP loans... _FRAUDULENTLY_! We uncovered HUNDREDS of loans in our analysis, and the largest - by far - was a $6.4 million loan provided to the Michigan Education Association. Not only were labor unions INELIGIBLE for these funds, but they had little need for them! The federal government allocated nearly **_$200 BILLION_** to public schools during the pandemic. These billions of dollars included money to pay union dues! [**When you read this exposé about the labor unions, PPP, and the $36 million in illegal loans - you'll be SHOCKED by the level of fraud committed on such a grand scale.**](http://trk.libertyledger._c291cmNlX2NvZGU9TExPMzU2MDEifQ/Duq5GCJSaZeMY3ImBQKd5g) As the CEO of Freedom Foundation, my mission is to show liberty-loving Americans the truth about labor unions and how they've become such an insidious influence in our nation. If we're going to bring about a new era of American prosperity, we must expose unions for what they are - the root cause of every growing national dysfunction within our borders. [**I hope you'll download this free report -_Profiting from a Pandemic_ \- to learn more about the level of deceit and fraud within labor unions across the country.**](http://trk.libertyledger._c291cmNlX2NvZGU9TExPMzU2MDEifQ/q9PZ2uya2F8eYWAWDsjNLw)
---

clean_body: | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. | | | | Fellow American, While small business owners were struggling to keep their doors open in 2020, labor unions were busy profiting from the pandemic. Yes -- that's right, your tax dollars went into the hands of union bosses to the tune of MILLIONS. Freedom Foundation has exposed hundreds of unions around the country that submitted fraudulent applications and robbed American taxpayers of $36 million in Paycheck Protection Program loans. I want to ensure that as many freedom-loving Americans as possible know the truth about these labor unions - truths that the liberal media and Big Tech want to keep under wraps! As you probably remember, at the height of the pandemic, Congress created the Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) for small businesses and nonprofit organizations to cover payroll, rent, and utility payments to ensure they could pay their employees while being forced to close their doors. But labor unions - including teachers' unions - saw PPP as a cash cow, despite being ineligible to apply for the loans. For decades, corrupt union bosses have ripped off workers with mandatory "dues," while rigging the system in their favor, so it should come as no surprise that hundreds of unions had no problem committing mass fraud against the federal government. At the end of the day, all the union bosses care about is lining their deep pockets to prop up the left's progressive machinery and push a radical, unconstitutional agenda. Fellow American, at least a dozen teachers' unions and advocacy groups received PPP loans... FRAUDULENTLY! We uncovered HUNDREDS of loans in our analysis, and the largest - by far - was a $6.4 million loan provided to the Michigan Education Association. Not only were labor unions INELIGIBLE for these funds, but they had little need for them! The federal government allocated nearly $200 BILLION to public schools during the pandemic. These billions of dollars included money to pay union dues! As the CEO of Freedom Foundation, my mission is to show liberty-loving Americans the truth about labor unions and how they've become such an insidious influence in our nation. If we're going to bring about a new era of American prosperity, we must expose unions for what they are - the root cause of every growing national dysfunction within our borders. In freedom, Aaron WitheCEOFreedom Foundation PO Box 552 Olympia WA, 98507 Liberty Ledger P.O. Box 455 Leesburg, VA 20178If you no longer wish to receive sponsored promotional emails from us to unsubscribe.

| Friend
One of the greatest obstacles standing in the way of progress are the career politicians we have in D.C.
Term limits are the only way we can keep our government transparent and accountable, and that is why I am honored to have received special recognition from the U.S. Term Limits organization.
