| At the Inauguration of the Governor, Tina Ramirez had no idea my aide was sitting right behind her and overheard her conversation.
I was dumbfounded at the tall tales she told to those who were sitting near her. Maybe it's because she knows she doesn't have a record to stand on so she has to tell a tall tale.
This section is dedicated to setting the record straight. Welcome to the first edition of Tina's Tall Tales.
---
|

| TINA TALES #1
My desperate opponent Tina Ramirez this week falsely claimed she's a small business owner but the truth is she runs a non-profit.
BIG DIFFERENCE
Non-profits ask for money from donors. Business owners must earn their profits by selling their product or services. Our family has owned a number of businesses over the years to include the ones we own now. We understand the difference. Clearly, Tina doesn't.
She's running a non profit and supporting her family off of donations. With a small business you typically have skin in the game and use your own money, and your own risk.
Non profit is simply spending other peoples' money or subsidies of the government's money that you and I have had taken from us in the form of taxes. So you and I are paying for her non profit to employee her family.
Non-profits do good work, but they are not businesses.

clean_body: From the Desk of Senator Chase | | | | | | == | CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 5 of 7 | | | | | | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK? | WEEK 5: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE | | | Senator Amanda Chase Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She has been rated as one of the most conservative Senator in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA. She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans. | Democrats Continued the Gun Grab | 2023 SESSION WEEK 5 OVERVIEW Crossover Week Dear Friends, The 2 days prior to "cross over" are the longest and hardest, as the Virginia Senate must finish voting on all bills before Tuesday midnight. Other than amendments to the budget, all of our work on Senate bills must come to completion because Wednesday, February 8, the Senate begins hearing any House bills that made it out of the Republican controlled House. Now what kind of bills do you think passed out of the Republican controlled Senate? And what kind of bills do you think passed the Democrat controlled Senate? I think you get the picture. I wouldn't be surprised if most bills that passed the House will die in the Senate and most bills that made it out of the Senate will die in the House. We're pretty much canceling each other out. While this doesn't seem productive, I'm thankful that many of these bad Senate bills will die in the Republican controlled House. Remember when the "Democrat Trifecta" passed and signed into law every liberal bill they could get through for a 2 year period? I'm so thankful that when a bad Senate bill passes the Senate, it will most likely die. Unfortunately, that peace of mind expires this year, as we're all up for re-election. That's 140 seats. 100 in the House and 40 in the Senate. All seats are in newly drawn districts in which many incumbent senators will actually face off against fellow incumbents or face retirement. While I'm thankful that I'm the only incumbent with an address in Senate District 12, both of my opponents Tina Ramirez and Glenn Sturtevant are not explaining to voters that they are running against one of the most conservative Republicans in the Virginia Senate. While I believe the seat belongs to the people, not politicians; it is important that we spend our resources against Democrats not incumbent Republicans who have a history of being re-elected. That said, I do support term limits. I have introduced term limit legislation every year for the 8 years I've been in office. But other self-serving politicians kill this bill every year. So it doesn't bother me that at least half of the Senate will turnover this year. In fact, when we return in January 2024 the entire General Assembly will experience a great reset. Many of our more senior members have already announced they are retiring, like Democrat Senators Dick Saslaw and Janet Howell. So has Republican Jill Vogel. Personally, I'm really looking forward to the New Virginia Senate after what I call the "Great Reset of the General Assembly." We need new blood in the General Assembly. That said, we must keep the House and win back the Senate. This Tuesday, June 20 I humbly ask you to vote for me in the Republican Primary and again on Tuesday, November 7 in the General Election. Elections have consequences and determine your quality of life and freedoms. You can trust me. You know my 8 year solid conservative voting record and you know I stand up for the people of my district. This week we voted on a number of 2nd Amendment bills. So, this week I will highlight a number of gun bills that passed out of the Democrat controlled Senate and should be killed in the House. We also battled through budget amendments and Governor's appointments. 3 of those appointments didn't make it through the Democrat controlled Virginia Senate. We've seen first hand that just because a Republican has an R by their name doesn't mean they're going to defend gun rights. In fact, we've seen an increasing number of "Republicans" vote for gun control. Now more than ever VOTING RECORDS MATTER. Currently, we have 3 Republican Senators that sometimes vote with Democrats on gun control measures. That's 3 Republican Senators out of 18 or 17% of our Republican Senators who vote for gun control. And now, my former seatmate who voted for anti-American red flag laws and lost the seat in 2019, wants to run against me. Gun owners can't trust Glenn Sturtevant. He's already proven we can't count on him. This week, just look at how much gun bills were voted on. Let me summarize for you some of the bad Senate gun bills that made it out of the Senate. I voted against all bills that are a direct threat to the 2nd Amendment SB1382- assault weapon and magazine ban Democrat Senator Creigh Deeds introduced == which targets "assault firearms" and certain ammunition feeding devices; purchase, possession, etc., prohibited. SB1382 creates a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person who imports, sells, manufactures, purchases, possesses, transports, or transfers an assault firearm, as that term is defined in the bill, and prohibits a person who has been convicted of such violation from purchasing, possessing, or transporting a firearm for a period of three years from the date of conviction. The bill provides that an assault firearm does not include any firearm that is an antique firearm, has been rendered permanently inoperable, is manually operated by bolt, pump, lever, or slide action, or was manufactured before July 1, 2023. The bill also prohibits the sale of a large capacity ammunition feeding device, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill provides that any person who willfully and intentionally (i) sells an assault firearm to another person or (ii) purchases an assault firearm from another person is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor and that any person who imports, sells, barters, or transfers a large capacity ammunition feeding device is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. The bill also makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person younger than 21 years of age to import, sell, manufacture, purchase, possess, transport, or transfer an assault firearm regardless of the date of manufacture of such assault firearm. Wait a minute. So you can serve in the Armed Forces at age 18 and learn how to use assault firearms but when you return home, you lose that right? This makes no sense. Here's the vote. floor: 01/31/23 Senate: Read third time and passed Senate (23-Y 16-N) YEAS--Barker, Bell, Boysko, Deeds, Dunnavant, Ebbin, Edwards, Favola, Hashmi, Howell, Locke, Lucas, Marsden, Mason, McClellan, McPike, Morrissey, Norment, Petersen, Rouse, Saslaw, Spruill, Surovell--23. NAYS--Chase, Cosgrove, DeSteph, Hackworth, Hanger, Lewis, McDougle, Newman, Obenshain, Peake, Pillion, Reeves, Ruff, Stanley, Stuart, Suetterlein--16. RULE 36--0. NOT VOTING--Vogel--1. Thankfully this bill died in the House 5 to 3. SB1067 - Expansion of Red Flag Laws On Monday, the Senate voted 23-16 to == == by Democrat Senator Scott Surovell that would expand Virginia's red flag law. A red flag law is a common term for substantial risk orders. These orders allow law enforcement and prosecutors to petition courts to temporarily prohibit possession, purchase or transportation of firearms from people who have been deemed a risk to themselves or others. My problem with red flag laws is that they deny the accused due process for up to 10 days. SB 1067 Substantial risk orders; substantial risk factors and considerations. floor: 01/30/23 Senate: Read third time and passed Senate (23-Y 16-N) YEAS--Barker, Bell, Boysko, Deeds, Ebbin, Edwards, Favola, Hanger, Hashmi, Howell, Lewis, Locke, Lucas, Marsden, Mason, McClellan, McPike, Morrissey, Petersen, Rouse, Saslaw, Spruill, Surovell--23. NAYS--Chase, Cosgrove, DeSteph, Dunnavant, Hackworth, McDougle, Newman, Norment, Obenshain, Peake, Pillion, Reeves, Ruff, Stanley, Stuart, Suetterlein--16. RULE 36--0. NOT VOTING--Vogel--1. The only Senate gun-control bill still left alive is SB 1067, Surovell, which expands the reach of the Red Flag law. It just got assigned to the House Courts of Justice committee. It will probably get heard next week. The state Senate voted 22-17 for Democrat Senator Adam Ebbin's == along party lines to ==, homemade firearms without serial numbers typically assembled using 3D printers, or the components used to build one. SB 1181 Unfinished frames or receivers and unserialized firearms; sale, transfer, or purchase, penalty. The measure makes it ==, which carries up to a year in jail and a $2,500 fine, for those knowingly selling or offering to sell, transfer or buy them unless they are a federal firearms importer, dealer or manufacturer. There is also an exception if "the unfinished frame or receiver is required by federal law to be, and has been, imprinted with a serial number by a federal firearms importer, manufacturer, or dealer." Violators charged with any subsequent offense could face ==, which carries up to a 10-year sentence and a $100,000 fine. floor: 01/30/23 Senate: Read third time and passed Senate (22-Y 17-N) YEAS--Barker, Bell, Boysko, Deeds, Ebbin, Edwards, Favola, Hashmi, Howell, Lewis, Locke, Lucas, Marsden, Mason, McClellan, McPike, Morrissey, Petersen, Rouse, Saslaw, Spruill, Surovell--22. NAYS--Chase, Cosgrove, DeSteph, Dunnavant, Hackworth, Hanger, McDougle, Newman, Norment, Obenshain, Peake, Pillion, Reeves, Ruff, Stanley, Stuart, Suetterlein--17. RULE 36--0. NOT VOTING--Vogel--1. SB 1181, Ebbin, requires homemade guns to be serialized - defeated 6 to 3 SB1192 - Assault Weapon and Magazine Ban The Senate also passed a bill == that prohibits people from carrying certain assault weapons in public on a 21-18 vote, with state Sen. Lynwood W. Lewis Jr., D-Accomack, siding with Republicans in opposition. The legislation would ban certain semi-automatic center-fire rifles, pistols, and shotguns in public spaces, including streets, sidewalks and parks. Under Virginia law, the ban on guns in public spaces is only in certain localities and applies to certain loaded guns. The bill would apply to firearms that are unloaded and expand the group of semi-automatic center-fire rifles, pistols and shotguns banned in public. Among other criteria, the proposal would remove the requirement that the banned weapon have a magazine capable of holding more than 20 rounds of ammunition or designed to include a silencer or "equipped with a folding stock." SB 1192 Assault firearms; carrying in public areas prohibited, penalty. floor: 01/30/23 Senate: Read third time and passed Senate (21-Y 18-N) YEAS--Barker, Bell, Boysko, Deeds, Ebbin, Edwards, Favola, Hashmi, Howell, Locke, Lucas, Marsden, Mason, McClellan, McPike, Morrissey, Petersen, Rouse, Saslaw, Spruill, Surovell--21. NAYS--Chase, Cosgrove, DeSteph, Dunnavant, Hackworth, Hanger, Lewis, McDougle, Newman, Norment, Obenshain, Peake, Pillion, Reeves, Ruff, Stanley, Stuart, Suetterlein--18. RULE 36--0. NOT VOTING--Vogel--1. SB1139 - requires guns to be stored in locked containers if there is someone under the age of 18 in the house Democrats in the state Senate advanced a== == by state Senator Jennifer Boysko (D) that would require adults to keep guns locked up if minors are in the home. The bill passed the Senate 22-Y 16-N with the help of Republican Senator Siobhan Dunnavant. Violations of the proposed law, which would require gun dealers to post notices at their stores informing customers about the consequences of improper gun storage, would be a misdemeanor offense punishable by fines of up to $250. SB 1139 Firearms; storage in residence where minor present, penalty. floor: 01/31/23 Senate: Read third time and passed Senate (22-Y 16-N) YEAS\--Barker, Bell, Boysko, Deeds, Dunnavant, Ebbin, Edwards, Favola, Hashmi, Howell, Locke, Lucas, Marsden, Mason, McClellan, McPike, Morrissey, Petersen, Rouse, Saslaw, Spruill, Surovell--22. NAYS--Chase, Cosgrove, DeSteph, Hackworth, Hanger, Lewis, McDougle, Newman, Obenshain, Peake, Pillion, Reeves, Ruff, Stanley, Stuart, Suetterlein--16. RULE 36--0. NOT VOTING--Norment, Vogel--2. SB 1139, Boysko, requires guns to be stored in locked containers if there is someone under the age of 18 in the house - defeated 5 to 3 Clearly the Democrats and even a few Republicans can't be trusted with your guns. | | | | | | | PRESS INTERVIEW WTVR CHANNEL 6 WITH JOI FULTZ SB1523 Why are Marijuana Felons Getting Automatic Resentencing? | | | == | | Wednesday Crossover. What you need to know. | == | Why I voted against the Virginia Budget | == | Love to See You at the Capitol | | | | | JOIN US MARCH 6 | | WITH YOUR GREAT HELP WE DID IT! FIRST PLACE IN FUNDRAISING | | Please Sign The Petition to Get My Name on the Ballot | == | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get at least 250 registered voters who live in == to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. If you'd like to sign my petition, you can stop by my senate office, see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events like our Tea Party meetings, held on the first of each month. We can also email you the form. You can ask family members to sign the petition and mail the form back to us, but please remember to get the form notarized at your bank before you mail it back to us. We must turn in our signatures before Monday, March 20 at noon sharp to the GOP District Chair. Please reach out to Mike at Mike@chaseforva.com. | DO YOU HAVE A GOOD SIGN LOCATION? | | Mike and I will begin putting up the first round of 4 by 8s tomorrow, Saturday February 10 and continue until all of our 4 by 8s have been placed. Please reach out to Mike if you have a good 4 by 8 location on a main road or want to help put up 4 by 8s at mike@chaseforva.com. Thanks to all who've already provided such great locations on main thoroughfares throughout Chesterfield and Colonial Heights. The primary is Tuesday, June 8. Beginning Wednesday February 15, yard signs are also available for pick up at these location during their business hours: Bob Moate's Sports Shop 10418 Hull Street Road Midlothian, VA 23112 Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30-6pm Friday 9-7pm Saturday 8-5pm Sunday 8-4pm Dance's Sporting Goods 570 Southpark Blvd Colonial Heights, VA 23834 Hours: Weekdays 8am to 6pm Saturday 10-4pm Sunday 12-4pm If you'd like to add your business as a pick up location, please reach out to Mike at mike@chaseforva.com | SETTING THE RECORD STRAIGHT Desperate Tina Ramirez There she goes again... | At the Inauguration of the Governor, Tina Ramirez had no idea my aide was sitting right behind her and overheard her conversation. I was dumbfounded at the tall tales she told to those who were sitting near her. Maybe it's because she knows she doesn't have a record to stand on so she has to tell a tall tale. This section is dedicated to setting the record straight. Welcome to the first edition of Tina's Tall Tales. | | TINA TALES #1 My desperate opponent Tina Ramirez this week falsely claimed she's a small business owner but the truth is she runs a non-profit. BIG DIFFERENCE Non-profits ask for money from donors. Business owners must earn their profits by selling their product or services. Our family has owned a number of businesses over the years to include the ones we own now. We understand the difference. Clearly, Tina doesn't. She's running a non profit and supporting her family off of donations. With a small business you typically have skin in the game and use your own money, and your own risk. Non profit is simply spending other peoples' money or subsidies of the government's money that you and I have had taken from us in the form of taxes. So you and I are paying for her non profit to employee her family. Non-profits do good work, but they are not businesses. | You are invited to come to the Capitol | There are links at the bottom of the newsletter that will help you follow various bills, legislators, committees and how to reach my office. If you'd like to stop by the General Assembly, I encourage you to reach out to my office below. We'd love to have you. If you call in advance we will do our best to find a time that I can personally meet with you. Also, if you'd like to help out, we're looking for people to collect signatures so that I can get my name on the ballot. I need 250 signatures. If you're interested, please reach out to Debbie at 804 393 2715. We hope to see you soon! | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Speaker Emerita Nancy Pelosi is the U.S. Representative for California’s 11th District. She's fighting to help working families and protect America from the dangerous agenda of Mitch McConnell and Kevin McCarthy. name: Derek Kilmer
subject: Nobody should have to ration life-saving medication
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Every day, I hear too many stories about people rationing their insulin, discontinuing prescription drugs, or going into debt because of the outrageous costs of life-saving medication.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58563706/391489665/-2027002553?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODQyMjFlODYtOTdhOS1lZDExLTk5NGQtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=uBvs4LEmgel6TeOJ5R3vbW7RalR2UiwoGVqFmCwNCcU=&emci=7b55a07f-97a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=84221e86-97a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=3595835)
&anbsp;
Peter,

Late last year, I was proud to support the Inflation Reduction Act. With this new law, we've capped the cost of insulin at $35 a month for seniors on Medicare.

**Still, Americans pay the highest prices for prescription drugs in the world.** And every day, I hear too many stories about people rationing their insulin, discontinuing prescription drugs, or going into debt because of the outrageous costs of life-saving medication.

Look, lowering prescription drug prices won't just help seniors; it also cuts the federal deficit by BILLIONS. In his State of the Union, President Biden made it clear that he will veto any attempts to raise drug prices.

The cost of insulin has created an affordability crisis for the 8.6% of Washingtonians living with diabetes. Meanwhile, the average price for a unit of insulin is **$98.70** , which is about **ten times higher** compared to other developed nations.

_This is unacceptable, and the time to act is NOW._ Let's finish the job and pass a national $35 insulin cap for all Americans, and bring down the costs of prescription drugs across the board.

**[The Inflation Reduction Act was a significant step forward in cutting costs and saving lives. But our work is not yet over. If you agree that it's time to cap insulin at $35 and lower the cost of prescription drugs, sign our petition to urge Congress to act by adding your name next to mine.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58563707/391489666/441830121?ms=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&refcode=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&source=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=gLtYl6IwM%2BRDcDl1NEnJ8jLA7aBNQuC3eSL1bLgnhUDhc23DT9g55mWqfec6rOzn%2FSuSQQNQ1SBDi8D2pbmyAXsQuAm%2Fhvspjy43mUYTcjVejOJPQ1NcoiWveDC8YoTXwaqq%2B0Bpt8ma2P9iCAI0fysMyvlaqMaZO%2Fz%2FembR3po%3D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODQyMjFlODYtOTdhOS1lZDExLTk5NGQtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=uBvs4LEmgel6TeOJ5R3vbW7RalR2UiwoGVqFmCwNCcU=&emci=7b55a07f-97a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=84221e86-97a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=3595835)**
[SIGN THE PETITION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58563708/391489667/441830121?ms=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&refcode=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&source=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230210_rxcap_gotv_gq_act___DK_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=gLtYl6IwM%2BRDcDl1NEnJ8jLA7aBNQuC3eSL1bLgnhUDhc23DT9g55mWqfec6rOzn%2FSuSQQNQ1SBDi8D2pbmyAXsQuAm%2Fhvspjy43mUYTcjVejOJPQ1NcoiWveDC8YoTXwaqq%2B0Bpt8ma2P9iCAI0fysMyvlaqMaZO%2Fz%2FembR3po%3D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODQyMjFlODYtOTdhOS1lZDExLTk5NGQtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=uBvs4LEmgel6TeOJ5R3vbW7RalR2UiwoGVqFmCwNCcU=&emci=7b55a07f-97a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=84221e86-97a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=3595835)
Thanks for your support – it's an honor to represent you.

Derek

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: I’m up for re-election and need your help, friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by AB PAC
body:
| | |
---
I’m up for re-election in 2024, friend, and as one of just three Democrats in states Trump won and the only remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio, I’m a prime target for Mitch McConnell and the GOP. _(__There's already a Republican vying for the GOP nomination.)_
NBC says my race could be “even nastier and more expensive” than last year’s Senate race in Ohio – and that race was one of the most expensive in the country with over $200 million spent.
**[Frankly, it’s going to be my toughest race yet, but if we work together, we can win in Ohio. Will you split a contribution between my campaign and American Bridge PAC as we begin the new year?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58544296/391446580/-1708609261?&amounts=15,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2246-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Nzc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRhNjNlMTZiLTZkYTktZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=0SNwOiXVT_2HUQhyhvC9auknYzjMqIJ7ASb7JeIKdhU=)**
I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio. I went to school with the sons and daughters of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. Eventually, those jobs disappeared as one big plant after another left town. Every day, I fight in the U.S. Senate to bring those jobs back to our state and to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families.
I wear a canary pin that a steelworker gave to me years ago – a reminder that workers used to have to take a canary with them to the coal mines in case there was dangerous gas. The pin is a symbol of the progress we’ve made on workers’ rights and the work we still have left to do.
Like raising wages. Making sure everyone has good job opportunities in their hometowns. Rewarding companies that invest in American jobs instead of shipping them overseas. Ensuring no one needs a second or third job just to get by.
That’s what the Dignity of Work is all about, friend. No matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do – whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips -- your work has dignity and I’ll fight for you.
[**I need you in this fight, too friend. It won’t be easy, but if we organize, work hard, and start early, I know we can do this. Please consider chipping in $15 – or whatever amount makes the most sense for you – to my campaign and American Bridge PAC.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58544297/391446581/-1708609261?&amounts=15,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2246-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Nzc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRhNjNlMTZiLTZkYTktZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=0SNwOiXVT_2HUQhyhvC9auknYzjMqIJ7ASb7JeIKdhU=)
__If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Sherrod Brown and American Bridge PAC will go through immediately:__ | | | [Donate $15](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58544298/391446582/-1708609261?&amounts=15,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=jtk2246-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5Nzc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRhNjNlMTZiLTZkYTktZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=0SNwOiXVT_2HUQhyhvC9auknYzjMqIJ7ASb7JeIKdhU=)

clean_body: | | | I’m up for re-election in 2024, friend, and as one of just three Democrats in states Trump won and the only remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio, I’m a prime target for Mitch McConnell and the GOP. (There's already a Republican vying for the GOP nomination.) NBC says my race could be “even nastier and more expensive” than last year’s Senate race in Ohio – and that race was one of the most expensive in the country with over $200 million spent. = I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio. I went to school with the sons and daughters of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. Eventually, those jobs disappeared as one big plant after another left town. Every day, I fight in the U.S. Senate to bring those jobs back to our state and to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families. I wear a canary pin that a steelworker gave to me years ago – a reminder that workers used to have to take a canary with them to the coal mines in case there was dangerous gas. The pin is a symbol of the progress we’ve made on workers’ rights and the work we still have left to do. Like raising wages. Making sure everyone has good job opportunities in their hometowns. Rewarding companies that invest in American jobs instead of shipping them overseas. Ensuring no one needs a second or third job just to get by. That’s what the Dignity of Work is all about, friend. No matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do – whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips -- your work has dignity and I’ll fight for you. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Sherrod Brown and American Bridge PAC will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = With gratitude, Sherrod | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. | | Paid for by AB PAC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. American Bridge 21st Century 800 Maine Ave SW Suite 400 Washington, DC 20024 United States

name: Ryan Chamberlin Campaign
subject: Tomorrow is the DAY - are you coming?
body:
| | |
---

If you were going to donate some time to help Ryan win, this is the day!

We are asking everyone to meet at the Mojo Grill on SE 17th Street and Hwy 200 (Near Target) at 9:15AM on Saturday Feb 11th

( Let us know you're coming here - [_https://rebrand.ly/WalkwithRyan_](https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=4koMV&m=iNcSePDouQve0iI&b=cm6nwIhepcmBIjqJtop3Iw) _)_
[](https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=4koMV&m=iNcSePDouQve0iI&b=cm6nwIhepcmBIjqJtop3Iw)

**EVERYONE that attends will receive a RED – Ryan Chamberlin Campaign shirt** , and we will have a camera crew taking photos and videos of all of us.

[](https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=4koMV&m=iNcSePDouQve0iI&b=cm6nwIhepcmBIjqJtop3Iw)

So let’s show up!

Please click this link to let us know your shirt size, and we can in turn make sure you have all the information you’ll need to help walk as many homes as possible on the 11th.

Click here now and please invite someone to join you. [_https://rebrand.ly/WalkwithRyan_](https://clicks.aweber.com/y/ct/?l=4koMV&m=iNcSePDouQve0iI&b=cm6nwIhepcmBIjqJtop3Iw)

Ideally, we will only need a total of 2 people per car to cover the most ground.

We will see you there!

Sincerely,

Ryan

name: Zilvinas Silenas
subject: We’re bringing hope to the classroom, but we need your help
body:
[](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=fa13b5a8285247258a95579c2011fbf0&_e=aty2nL5TdgVCMxKVawlgNXzjoMQ_81zuX7ZzKylTua1RXc2RwvfdM-qMYS-L6DoLyozFv7jhey_2Pk1ZmK5Nrmz30jO2XvZTSoPvPKKdVFmILPhXAJcA_lfRkK1e8lm32FhuvMMrvcxc3tDaeEcNFE8Cn3p-Hb2dzT35vi0JN6d8vnciMEQqZR10xOxYXXt3nV_kiaIBgMWu9V22ZRNASgsvyy8IEEuvn43EfvoyL7WRJkHox3yFUmFLCMKIFyhdFfEnJmlUPEZg6YtHcEgvAjpLLldT3rfuaZA5b3R48mIyrmz8eOVUKsVzEaLkx-6e)

Hello Friend,

I'm very excited to share that the Foundation for Economic Education (FEE) is partnering with Detroit Public Schools to**teach liberty and bring hope to 3,500+ 10th and 11th-grade students this year. **

FEE Teacher Training and Revolution of One, in collaboration with the Michigan Council on Economic Education, established a partnership with the Detroit Public Schools Community District to assist with their social studies curriculum.

This is an important step in spreading our message of liberty and free markets to the next generation.

Since 2019, FEE's **Revolution of One** initiative has aimed to engage geographic areas and audiences that the freedom philosophy typically doesn't reach.

Let me introduce myself. My name is Zilvinas Silenas, President of FEE. Our mission is to make the ideas of liberty familiar, credible, and compelling to the rising generation.

That's why I've set a goal to raise $12,000 this week to support our work in schools -- in Detroit and throughout our country.

[_**Will you help FEE deliver liberty and hope in more public schools by sending a gift of $10, $25, $50, or any amount today?**__**Click here >>>**_](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=fa13b5a8285247258a95579c2011fbf0&_e=aty2nL5TdgVCMxKVawlgNXzjoMQ_81zuX7ZzKylTua1RXc2RwvfdM-qMYS-L6DoLyozFv7jhey_2Pk1ZmK5Nrmz30jO2XvZTSoPvPKKdVFmILPhXAJcA_lfRkK1e8lm32FhuvMMrvcxc3tDaeEcNFLjKvz8xhXmugGygpI5i8ZCWCNRDSwMFCnp4rY5vGLUvqoKOjvGWHwfOmUsXzLFqFiKASiA7GpBWBJ4gi6l5NVmMnJWmr6RqPDaEKQjHI8gF7C7r9PRqPZMrAZ_QMAJq9PSHIs2qbfs_BfAWvF3mdlkuNpTVC41ss4bT-E_esJEj)

Here's how we're reaching students in the Detroit Public Schools:

FEE secured a partnership with the Detroit Public Schools Community District to design and deliver an engaging curriculum that will inspire students to overcome their struggles and harness their strengths to create the life they most desire.

Economics teachers at 24 Detroit high schools will deliver the curriculum this Spring. It will include eight days of content that explores:
* The effects of big government and central planning programs such as "redlining"
* Other types of historical government-imposed economic discrimination
* The impacts of these programs, which still affect people today

As a component of the curriculum, a Revolution of One speaker will deliver a direct-to-student educational workshop entitled "The Comeback Kid."

This workshop will highlight the government's failures that caused the discriminatory "redlining" practices while empowering students to employ the ideas of liberty to escape the "victim mindset."

Ultimately, Revolution of One will help students process what they've learned, channel their frustrations about cruel historical economic realities, and inspire them to pursue freedom in their individual lives.

[_**Your generous support goes towards educating and empowering the next generation. Think about how many young lives you can change with a simple gift. Click here to give >>>**_](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=fa13b5a8285247258a95579c2011fbf0&_e=aty2nL5TdgVCMxKVawlgNXzjoMQ_81zuX7ZzKylTua1RXc2RwvfdM-qMYS-L6DoLyozFv7jhey_2Pk1ZmK5Nrmz30jO2XvZTSoPvPKKdVFmILPhXAJcA_lfRkK1e8lm32FhuvMMrvcxc3tDaeEcNFJCq9mDALH7KC03T5GXYd627juS6cF6neTorhIwTo-0E0ZK2IOOsuKegn2Ii2Xtoh0TMv-zn6scy0JqjEUIfe4EX2k6kA9u7V4uccEkVpIBiS3gYMULyMgVYVfSwicqngxQ-9IOP8CbYTokvtzLT7Hdrdr0narQ5JlPjHFdeC8oJ)

Soon, 24 high schools in Detroit will teach FEE's curriculum to high school sophomores and juniors.

**An estimated 3,500-5,000students will hear from FEE about how to overcome the obstacles built by government intervention in the market and achieve their dreams.**

And most importantly, we're able to share hope -- hope that our country isn't evil, hope that lines of division will disappear, and hope for a better tomorrow.

But FEE can't do it alone. That's why we partner with school systems like Detroit and individuals who believe in liberty and free markets.

That's why I want to partner with you, Friend.

Friend, can you help support hope and liberty in the classroom?

I hope you'll consider sending your support and help us meet our goal of raising $12,000 this week. **Click below to donate.**

[_**Donate $10 >>>**_](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=fa13b5a8285247258a95579c2011fbf0&_e=aty2nL5TdgVCMxKVawlgNXzjoMQ_81zuX7ZzKylTua1RXc2RwvfdM-qMYS-L6DoLyozFv7jhey_2Pk1ZmK5Nrmz30jO2XvZTSoPvPKKdVFmILPhXAJcA_lfRkK1e8lm39Kg_TF42b2Sr2EeZN71dGeeVkmlM6Cz8XttGbXzbld71uYY_xP13N0fZWXJGCR-cEXdhEeC16Lga1kZreG4MYPHOoExum9FaIuCRdHGJxaBf14WnHl6AnFVU8T3FLlY88GwmHDKKkONCGO6n5A1PiZyX-KmtY-sRGM7QHst9J4AkJxIXWjjfOIVIoff-j-GC)

The deadline to fill out the survey is at midnight tonight, Friend. If you fail to take the survey before the deadline, our data could be tainted. WHICH ISSUE DO YOU CARE ABOUT MOST? Thank you, Elise Stefanik House GOP Conference Chair Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC. Team Elise, P.O. [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=5e2d184dcc5247fe99158eb51bd6e78d&_e=RNdoGFTPRZmot9j2qTVCuc9xpa2njWVy28a7RjtEtyWEIykURrCJA1gWtiRQ3USsKxXyQoTWLUcDKhghifvdFw9h2lFHbDd5JixmhxZ7EPieQVWkOLj73pOskAYIfFgtDHn0gCzPcYOIRdra-pfVU9Mp7p8kkirWOIjEsioJFXV2taY5Z3M8RHzJgbme-gEIR2PbxtA6NVpWikyoKkP3ODG0l2h7S0gtSthFFuXjCxIUgyx08b-9FJVeVY-a6TM38Nhk0BDyc4FaYL1yOCJR7bCzIZnEG24HlsdbyD4pXmw%3D)

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: one minute
body:
Republicans only need to win two more seats to deliver Senate control back to Mitch McConnell.
| | |
---
Derek, this will only take a minute of your time:
Tonight marks one of our first big fundraising deadlines since I officially launched my reelection campaign. But right now, we're falling way short of our $30,000 end-of-week goal, so I'm counting on your help to close this gap ASAP.
**Republicans only need to win two more seats to deliver Senate control back to Mitch McConnell.** And here in New York, they just had their best election cycle in two decades — winning enough seats to deliver the House majority to the GOP. Now, they're zeroing in on my seat to flip the Senate next.
Missing this major goal would send a message to McConnell and the GOP that this seat is vulnerable — plus, it would mean starting this campaign at a serious fundraising disadvantage. I don't take any money from corporate PACs, Derek, so I can't reach this goal in time without your urgent grassroots support.

name: Mike Lee
subject: SOTU: ‘not well’
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
this is a blatant mischaracterization of what we believe.
Fellow American - you might have seen my reaction during the State of the Union.

I was shocked. _And it made the rounds on the internet:_

[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=39680e0d64144f0cad212986a2b3acff&_e=pLmPlVMX4i1k8kCkK_5ASk7A19pC6WZ3CAfQO2eAya4FEQQtoyV9sGSVIgkjQclEi49JXgQ2xrVmN739trkLEbm8PEeK2LW63le1jNnS8RUn9Vp_f22cjjsS13O1sVscflxBc8xqfqp2aLBiO2tBiTAqMTpMux3tAqGtPmGeqglRH7TOvpols-vFfdOxbwOJukpYXxdT_UxkpJtxqyMOsJyqz95bGPqACFOmRgtRr6p9-TBe7LNzT8zPa3-DSQrDCqXABxMqL9Q9PbxNg1LgA-CsRtOtYqUiQOxKCNQaO-nZL4NgU1g4wb5Mbfv21pbn5v8O5lVXiO_dn-gwhf5bH4f7x3fAF6j_UW56E2V5yDndbtqbwd2rME8lFrlbRWjsZG0EefvbGxkySN6Pwe06P5Fue061XbaYLFhuUUWxlGm0OoI8nvIDThYlXnuP6ksU)
Here's what stunned me: the President of the United States looked us right in the eye and mischaracterized what half the people in the chamber believe.

Fellow American, I don't know about you, but I am sick and tired of this charade from the Left. They control Big Tech and the mainstream media. They want to censor what we're saying while propping up Joe Biden.

name: Indiana Republican Party
subject: On the State of the Union
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
The Party Line — An Indiana Republican Party Newsletter
| | |
---
|
---
# _On the State of the Union_
---
Well, that was a doozy. On Tuesday, President Biden gave his second State of the Union Address. And if you didn't watch it, you probably weren't the only one. According to one report, viewership for the address took a big nosedive this year, down 29 percent to just 27.3 million — the lowest viewership in 30 years. And it's no wonder why. Just two days prior to the address, an ABC poll found that 41 percent of Americans say that they're financially worse off since he took office — the highest percentage dating back nearly 40 years. With a number like that, and a president that continues to claim that he's building an economy "from the ground up and middle out" while wages, incomes, and savings decline, most Americans probably decided that they would rather watch science fiction than Biden's fiction. Thankfully, we have a Republican congress that is working every day to hold this administration accountable and deliver for the American people. And in 2024, we'll elect a Republican president and senate to work along side them. Enjoy this week's newsletter and share it with a friend. Sincerely,
---
---
Chairman Kyle Hupfer
---
|
---
| |
# **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**
---
| |
# _Appointed to the Council of Governors_
---
---
| | _**Governor Eric Holcomb** speaks during a event for Alzheimer's Association Advocacy Day at the statehouse earlier this week._
---
Yesterday, the White House announced that **Governor Eric Holcomb** will be appointed to the [Council of Governors](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/ca306c76976ab151c3d6b838967d376c/40313) for a two-year term. According to the [White House](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/10f4e79e4206a42dc3d6b838967d376c/40313): "The Council is comprised of 10 bipartisan governors from across the country and serves as the lead forum to increase coordination around preparedness, resilience, and response between the Federal government and state governments, and strengthen the Federal-State partnership that’s critical to protecting our nation from threats to our homeland security. The Council of Governors serves as the lead forum for communication and collaboration between the States and the Federal government on homeland defense, civil support, synchronization and integration of State and Federal military activities in the United States, and matters of mutual interest pertaining to the National Guard. This year, the Council will for the first-time discuss supply chain resiliency and Federal-State cooperation to mitigate risks to defense critical infrastructure." Other members of the Council of Governors include:
* Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine, Co-Chair
* Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, Co-Chair
* Delaware Gov. John Carney
* Louisiana Gov. John Bel Edwards
* Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer
* Utah Gov. Spencer Cox
* Vermont Gov. Phil Scott
* Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon

This week, **Gov. Holcomb** also had the chance to catch up with **[Sen. Todd Young](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/16fa986eb4ac2020c3d6b838967d376c/40313)** and **[Reps. Rudy Yakym and Erin Houchin](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/ceb5a5cc3eba3c4cc3d6b838967d376c/40313)** on Capitol Hill!
---
|
---
# _Become a Digital Member_
---
[](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/048aed745cf853cec3d6b838967d376c/40313)
---
We’re not letting up for a moment to elect Republicans up and down the ballot across Indiana. Our strong and united Indiana Republican Party got the job done to keep our great state **dark red** on November 8, 2022, and now is the time to prepare for the fights ahead. You can help us do just that by [signing up for a digital membership](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/b2a30d3bd5116416c3d6b838967d376c/40313) —you’ll receive a membership card in the mail and be an official part of our team of digital supporters that’s helping us keep Indiana a Republican stronghold.

|

# _Fox News: Biden's IRS plans to crack down on waiters' tips_
---
From [Fox News](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/49a55179cd09119bc3d6b838967d376c/40313): The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) proposed a revenue procedure this week to crack down on the service industry's reporting of tips. The Service Industry Tip Compliance Agreement (SITCA) program would be a voluntary tip reporting system in which the IRS and service industry companies cooperate, according to the announcement Monday. As part of the proposal, the IRS will give the public until early May to provide feedback on the program before implementing it. "Those 87,000 new IRS agents that you were promised would only target the rich," tweeted Mike Palicz, federal affairs manager at Americans for Tax Reform. "They're coming after waitresses' tips now." _Story continues._
---
| | |
---
# _Another Busy Week at the Statehouse_
---
Bills continue to move out of committees at the statehouse, while others have moved from one chamber to the other. _Priority Bills of House Republicans_ This week, [HB 1005](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/b79c39448900a7e5c3d6b838967d376c/40313) passed the chamber. HB 1005 the bill establishes a revolving loan fund to help municipalities cover a portion of the costs of housing development projects. _Priority Bills of Senate Republicans_ This week, [SB 2](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/287a93a778e95dadc3d6b838967d376c/40313), [SB 5](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/d2cb6a1ff2cc223cc3d6b838967d376c/40313), and [SB 7](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/41fcd7e709c29ecbc3d6b838967d376c/40313) passed the chamber. SB2 would allow small businesses to claim an unlimited deduction for state tax payments on their federal tax returns, which is currently limited to $10,000; the bill could result in an estimated $50 million in savings for small businesses. SB 5 would create a “bill of rights” for Hoosiers' data privacy which would allow them to monitor how their data is being used and have it deleted. SB 7 would ban physician non-compete agreements. Many other bills are moving. To stay up-to-date, visit [iga.in.gov](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/358049cffb424e9ec3d6b838967d376c/40313) and check back here next week.
---
|
---
# _Washington Examiner: Biden administration forced border officials to release 600,000 illegal immigrants_

---
After spending only 60 seconds talking about the crisis at southern border on Tuesday, President Biden probably doesn't want you to read this. From the [Washington Examiner](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/fb11bb87e6bdb90c/21e6b4aaeda6c779c3d6b838967d376c/40313): The Biden administration dramatically accelerated the rate at which it forced federal immigration officials at the border to release illegal immigrants into the country and did not place them in court proceedings. One year after the Washington Examiner reported that 47,000 illegal immigrants were released into the United States without being charged for the federal offense of unlawful entry or given a date to appear in immigration court to face justice, a new report from NBC News found that nearly 600,000 immigrants in total were discharged through this method in the last year. _Story continues._
---
|

clean_body: The Party Line — An Indiana Republican Party Newsletter | | | | On the State of the Union Well, that was a doozy. On Tuesday, President Biden gave his second State of the Union Address. And if you didn't watch it, you probably weren't the only one. According to one report, viewership for the address took a big nosedive this year, down 29 percent to just 27.3 million — the lowest viewership in 30 years. And it's no wonder why. Just two days prior to the address, an ABC poll found that 41 percent of Americans say that they're financially worse off since he took office — the highest percentage dating back nearly 40 years. With a number like that, and a president that continues to claim that he's building an economy "from the ground up and middle out" while wages, incomes, and savings decline, most Americans probably decided that they would rather watch science fiction than Biden's fiction. Thankfully, we have a Republican congress that is working every day to hold this administration accountable and deliver for the American people. And in 2024, we'll elect a Republican president and senate to work along side them. Enjoy this week's newsletter and share it with a friend. Sincerely, Chairman Kyle Hupfer | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Appointed to the Council of Governors | | Governor Eric Holcomb speaks during a event for Alzheimer's Association Advocacy Day at the statehouse earlier this week. Yesterday, the White House announced that Governor Eric Holcomb will be appointed to the for a two-year term. According to the : "The Council is comprised of 10 bipartisan governors from across the country and serves as the lead forum to increase coordination around preparedness, resilience, and response between the Federal government and state governments, and strengthen the Federal-State partnership that’s critical to protecting our nation from threats to our homeland security. The Council of Governors serves as the lead forum for communication and collaboration between the States and the Federal government on homeland defense, civil support, synchronization and integration of State and Federal military activities in the United States, and matters of mutual interest pertaining to the National Guard. This year, the Council will for the first-time discuss supply chain resiliency and Federal-State cooperation to mitigate risks to defense critical infrastructure." Other members of the Council of Governors include: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine, Co-Chair Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz, Co-Chair Delaware Gov. John Carney Louisiana Gov. John Bel Edwards Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer Utah Gov. Spencer Cox Vermont Gov. Phil Scott Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon This week, Gov. Holcomb also had the chance to catch up with and on Capitol Hill! | Become a Digital Member We’re not letting up for a moment to elect Republicans up and down the ballot across Indiana. Our strong and united Indiana Republican Party got the job done to keep our great state dark red on November 8, 2022, and now is the time to prepare for the fights ahead. You can help us do just that by —you’ll receive a membership card in the mail and be an official part of our team of digital supporters that’s helping us keep Indiana a Republican stronghold. | Fox News: Biden's IRS plans to crack down on waiters' tips From : The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) proposed a revenue procedure this week to crack down on the service industry's reporting of tips. The Service Industry Tip Compliance Agreement (SITCA) program would be a voluntary tip reporting system in which the IRS and service industry companies cooperate, according to the announcement Monday. As part of the proposal, the IRS will give the public until early May to provide feedback on the program before implementing it. "Those 87,000 new IRS agents that you were promised would only target the rich," tweeted Mike Palicz, federal affairs manager at Americans for Tax Reform. "They're coming after waitresses' tips now." Story continues. | | | Another Busy Week at the Statehouse Bills continue to move out of committees at the statehouse, while others have moved from one chamber to the other. Priority Bills of House Republicans This week, passed the chamber. HB 1005 the bill establishes a revolving loan fund to help municipalities cover a portion of the costs of housing development projects. Priority Bills of Senate Republicans This week, , , and passed the chamber. SB2 would allow small businesses to claim an unlimited deduction for state tax payments on their federal tax returns, which is currently limited to $10,000; the bill could result in an estimated $50 million in savings for small businesses. SB 5 would create a “bill of rights” for Hoosiers' data privacy which would allow them to monitor how their data is being used and have it deleted. SB 7 would ban physician non-compete agreements. Many other bills are moving. To stay up-to-date, visit and check back here next week. | Washington Examiner: Biden administration forced border officials to release 600,000 illegal immigrants After spending only 60 seconds talking about the crisis at southern border on Tuesday, President Biden probably doesn't want you to read this. From the : The Biden administration dramatically accelerated the rate at which it forced federal immigration officials at the border to release illegal immigrants into the country and did not place them in court proceedings. One year after the Washington Examiner reported that 47,000 illegal immigrants were released into the United States without being charged for the federal offense of unlawful entry or given a date to appear in immigration court to face justice, a new report from NBC News found that nearly 600,000 immigrants in total were discharged through this method in the last year. Story continues. | Now Hiring Interns Do you know a college students that's interested in stepping into the world of Hoosier politics, we've got them covered. We're looking for young, talented Hoosiers to join us at our headquarters in Indianapolis. This opportunity allows them to get hands-on experience working for Indiana's largest and most successful political organization, providing administrative support to staff and taking on special projects (and more) while helping build their resume, gain insight into the political and legislative processes, and make valuable connections. And we'll do this all while helping them earn college credit. Have someone in mind? clean_body: Will you help shape our strategy for this next cycle by representing | | | | | Hi there: I'm Heather Williams, interim president of the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee. Right now is a critical time for our organization: we're building our strategy for 2023, 2024, and beyond, deciding where and how we invest, identifying key seats and chambers, and so much more. It’s important to me that our plans for the future reflect the priorities of our most active grassroots supporters – so I wanted to take a moment to reach out to you today: Last November, state Democrats defied all odds, flipping key chambers, securing new trifectas, and defending our majorities in an ultra-competitive midterm year. Our grassroots supporters are the core of our movement – without the support of committed Democrats like you, none of these victories would have been possible. That’s why your input is so crucial. Supporters like you play a decisive role in each and every race – and the first step in helping Democratic candidates win elected office is sharing more about what matters to you. | Survey ID: Status: Not submitted Due: **11:59 PM Custom link: Thanks for taking the time to share your input! name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Will you help me prepare for 2024, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
Ohio is already shaping up to be a must-win battleground.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58548112/391458635/-880503781?refcode=BLU-FFP-20230210-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNlODM3ZjI4LTc5YTktZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=P-el3X0LNk7XuUmGviJVOiZqptO70sONhOzSWxYGrgo=)
---
I’m up for re-election in 2024, Peter, and as one of just three Democrats in states Trump won and the only remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio, I’m a prime target for Mitch McConnell and the GOP. _(There’s already a wealthy Republican state senator considering running against me.)_

NBC says my race could be “even nastier and more expensive” than this year’s Senate race in Ohio -- and that race was one of the most expensive in the country with over $200 million spent.

In fact, 2023 has just begun and already an opponent has jumped into the race and the NRSC is running attack ads against me.

[_**Peter, this is going to be my toughest race yet, but if we work together, we can win in Ohio. Will you split a contribution between my campaign and Fight for Progress as we begin the new year?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58548113/391458636/-880503781?refcode=BLU-FFP-20230210-1-EM&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNlODM3ZjI4LTc5YTktZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=P-el3X0LNk7XuUmGviJVOiZqptO70sONhOzSWxYGrgo=)

I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio. I went to school with the sons and daughters of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. Eventually, those jobs disappeared as one big plant after another left town. Every day, I fight in the U.S. Senate to bring those jobs back to our state and to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families.

I wear a canary pin that a steelworker gave to me years ago – a reminder that workers used to have to take a canary with them to the coal mines in case there was dangerous gas. The pin is a symbol of the progress we’ve made on workers’ rights and the work we still have left to do.

Like raising wages. Making sure everyone has good job opportunities in their hometowns. Rewarding companies that invest in American jobs instead of shipping them overseas. Ensuring no one needs a second or third job just to get by.

That’s what the Dignity of Work is all about, Peter. No matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do -- whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips -- your work has dignity and I’ll fight for you.

[_**I need you in this fight, too, Peter. It won’t be easy, but if we organize, work hard, and start early, I know we can do this. Please consider chipping in our average online contribution of $26 -- or whatever amount makes the most sense for you.**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58548114/391458637/-880503781?refcode=BLU-FFP-20230210-1-EM&amount=26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImNlODM3ZjI4LTc5YTktZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=P-el3X0LNk7XuUmGviJVOiZqptO70sONhOzSWxYGrgo=)

With gratitude,

Sherrod

clean_body: Ohio is already shaping up to be a must-win battleground. | = I’m up for re-election in 2024, Peter, and as one of just three Democrats in states Trump won and the only remaining statewide-elected Democrat in Ohio, I’m a prime target for Mitch McConnell and the GOP. (There’s already a wealthy Republican state senator considering running against me.) NBC says my race could be “even nastier and more expensive” than this year’s Senate race in Ohio -- and that race was one of the most expensive in the country with over $200 million spent. In fact, 2023 has just begun and already an opponent has jumped into the race and the NRSC is running attack ads against me. = I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio. I went to school with the sons and daughters of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. Eventually, those jobs disappeared as one big plant after another left town. Every day, I fight in the U.S. Senate to bring those jobs back to our state and to put money back in the pockets of hardworking families. I wear a canary pin that a steelworker gave to me years ago – a reminder that workers used to have to take a canary with them to the coal mines in case there was dangerous gas. The pin is a symbol of the progress we’ve made on workers’ rights and the work we still have left to do. Like raising wages. Making sure everyone has good job opportunities in their hometowns. Rewarding companies that invest in American jobs instead of shipping them overseas. Ensuring no one needs a second or third job just to get by. That’s what the Dignity of Work is all about, Peter. No matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do -- whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips -- your work has dignity and I’ll fight for you. = With gratitude, Sherrod * | | = Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee. Fight for Progress PAC PO Box 15320 Washington, DC 20003 United States | | ---|--- = = Emails are crucial for communicating with top members of the Fight for Progress PAC team. disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
Wisconsin has a once-in-a-decade opportunity to rebalance the Wisconsin state Supreme Court and buck Republican authoritarianism in Wisconsin once and for all. |
#
Last week, Pod Save America covered the stakes of this election. _Here’s what they said:_ Wisconsin has a once-in-a-decade opportunity to rebalance the Wisconsin State Supreme Court and buck Republican authoritarianism in Wisconsin once and for all. But there’s also a very real chance we get shut out of this April election if a progressive doesn’t make the cut in our fast-approaching primary on February 21. We stay neutral in the primary, Peter. But we can’t sit back and let MAGA candidates Jennifer Dorow and Dan Kelly capture all the votes this month. We only have 11 days to mobilize voters for progressives ahead of our primary. To do it, I need to make sure my team has the resources they need to beat Dorow and Kelly this month. [**So I’m personally asking you, Peter: Can you rush in any amount to support Tammy Baldwin and my team at the WisDems before our primary on February 21? tell us what’s important to you in the 2023 elections

|

Derek, It’s important that everyone’s voices are heard. This year, Virginia has some of the most critical races in the country and **we need all hands on deck.** But, the DPVA wants to know what is important to YOU. The only way we can know is by reaching out to everyone and getting their take on things. We’d like to hear from you, directly. **What is it that you find most important in 2023?** **Will you take 2 minutes to fill out our survey?** | **********[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58565041/391505751/-1237090878?refcode=dpva-em-230210-survey&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZjFjMzFlOS1hM2E5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dLKPc0BFc352SqdOPO54rC8a4Vn5X1ciCmv6IsDNnS0=&emci=07143252-8da9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=cf1c31e9-a3a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=15759664)**********

name: =?utf-8?Q?Andrew=20Roth?=
subject: The Battle of Lexington…South Carolina
body:
| | | |
---
| | | **Friends,**

The SC Freedom Caucus is holding strong, fighting the SC House GOP Caucus over the so-called “loyalty pledge”. To recap, South Carolina House members were forced to sign this pledge that would prohibit members from publicly calling out their more liberal colleagues when they undermine conservative principles. The SC Freedom Caucus opposed the pledge and refused to sign it. Their punishment was expulsion from the House GOP Caucus.

The pledge is basically a way for RINO Republicans to muzzle the SC Freedom Caucus. It’s an incumbent protection racket. Naturally, the conservative grassroots is siding with our brave freedom caucus members. With your ongoing support, they are relentlessly pressuring the Establishment in Columbia to ditch the pledge.

Now a major breakthrough has occurred. The Lexington County GOP has officially [passed](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=5616383963&e=64ecec6d03) a resolution that condemns the pledge. Lexington is home to some RINO incumbents, like State Rep. Micah Caskey, who have loudly fought the SC Freedom Caucus. Now his own neighbors are against his bullying tactics. Expect more counties to pile on. Momentum is with us!

Speaking of momentum, national conservative leaders [penned a letter](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=b6c145edd9&e=64ecec6d03) this week in support of the SC Freedom Caucus. Signers included Jim DeMint, Mark Meadows, the legendary Morton Blackwell, Noah Wall with FreedomWorks, Wade Miller with Citizens for Renewing America, and Bob Carlstrom with AMAC Action.

**AROUND THE COUNTRY** \- The Montana Senate just passed SB 99, a bill that would ban transgender surgeries for children! A huge victory that the **Montana Freedom Caucus** was a part of. Here’s a [short clip](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=f426bc94c0&e=64ecec6d03) of MTFC Chair State Senator Theresa Manzella speaking out in favor of the bill.

\- Another big fight is brewing in Montana. The GOP Establishment wants to reserve just a small portion of the state’s $3 billion surplus for tax relief and use the rest for big government spending programs. Opposing the plan is a top priority for the **Montana Freedom Caucus** , which wants to send most of it back while using the rest to pay down debt. No tax dollars will be used to grow government.

\- The **Wyoming Freedom Caucus** is [demanding answers](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=8df6a9e105&e=64ecec6d03) from Pfizer about their “gain of function” program that was recently exposed in an undercover video. In their letter to the company’s CEO, they write, “At a time when institutional trust in the pharmaceutical industry is at an all-time low, the Wyoming Freedom Caucus demands that Pfizer Inc. inform the people of Wyoming of the legitimacy of the claims in the video captured by Project Veritas.”

\- Democrats and Establishment Republicans are trying to pass a $102 million corporate welfare bill in the Gem State against the opposition of the **Idaho Freedom Caucus**. It [squeaked past](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=06257e35f1&e=64ecec6d03) the ID House, 26-24. The fight is now in the ID Senate.

\- The **Mississippi Freedom Caucus** along with the **South Dakota Freedom Caucus** renewed their demand that their state AGs withdraw from the corrupt National Association of Attorneys General (NAAG) over the group’s woke ESG agenda. In MSFC’s letter to MS Attorney General Lynn Fitch, they write, “If NAAG is investing in left-wing ESG campaigns behind your back, what else are they doing without your knowledge? What else is out of your control at NAAG? What else is the left-wing career staff in Washington, D.C. doing in conjunction with NAAG leadership from places like Oregon, New York, and Connecticut?” Breitbart has [the story](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=ed55d5abb5&e=64ecec6d03).

\- In response to a bill supported by Democrats and RINO Republicans to send more tax dollars to government-run schools, **Arizona Freedom Caucus** member State Rep. Alex Kolodin tweeted, “For years Arizonans have pumped more and more money into education ... but the funds never seem to make it to teacher pay and classroom funding. Yesterday the [@AZFreedomCaucus](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=bfea2c1666&e=64ecec6d03) fired a warning shot that the spigot has an expiration date unless taxpayer money is used PROPERLY!”

---
| [CLICK HERE TO DONATE](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=90c68c2299&e=64ecec6d03 "CLICK HERE TO DONATE")
---
| Please help us continue to support these brave members of the various state freedom caucuses. For just $10 a month, you can support the State Freedom Caucus Network as it works to fight big government and secure liberty at the state level. Click [here](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=ac7b44894e&e=64ecec6d03) to set up your recurring monthly donation now. Even $25 or $50 will make a huge difference.

Keep fighting,

**Andy Roth**
**President**
**State Freedom Caucus Network**

P.S. Your help is critical in building out a network of patriots and freedom fighters throughout our nation’s state capitols and in your home state. Please consider a [contribution here](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=fcdcd553af&e=64ecec6d03) and join us as we work to advance the cause of liberty.
---

You can [update your preferences](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/profile?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=7256df9da1&e=64ecec6d03&c=c3ef7e0ea2) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=7256df9da1&e=64ecec6d03&c=c3ef7e0ea2).

name: China Accountability Task Force (House GOP)
subject: Official 2023 China Accountability Survey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
| |
---
| | | Hello,

We hope this email finds you well. House Republicans are conducting a _**[formal survey](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r "formal
survey")**_ of a small handful of informed individuals on the biggest national security concern of our time:

**Communist China aggression. **

China has been in the news a lot recently for threatening American interests both abroad and right here at home. We launched this survey to get a better understanding on how the American People feel about recent developments - we chose you to represent your community.

This survey is just a few short questions and won't take more than a moment to complete. We promise. These questions are completely voluntary so only answer the questions you want to answer or feel strongly about.

[**We have attached your survey below this short introduction.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r "We have attached
your survey below this short introduction.")

Thank you for providing your input.

**China Accountability Task Force**
_From House Republicans HQ_

---
| **OFFICIAL 2023 CHINA ACCOUNTABILITY SURVEY
Survey active for: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r "dpwillis67@gmail.com")
Deadline to complete: 11:59 PM Tonight**
---
**_Question 1:_ China is buying up hundreds of thousands of acres of U.S. farmland - do you think Congress should BAN these purchases?****[**YES**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r) _[**NO**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r)**
* * *
**_Question 2:_ A Chinese spy balloon recently floated over the entire continental U.S. before Joe Biden finally took action- do you approve of his decision to wait an entire week?****[**YES**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r) _[**NO**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r)**
* * *
**_Question 3:_ Many Republicans criticized Biden for taking so long - do you think he should have shot down the Chinese spy balloon right when it crossed into U.S. airspace?****[**YES**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r) _[**NO**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310547:J8EUhDGND:m:1:3299561967:8E064B4F130AC9A2B32C04372963718B:r)**
