body:
>From filling former House minority leader Jim Durkin's seat with an independent to introducing a bill to allow illegal aliens to vote, Illinois Review

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/124f1f0a0e9a/alert-conservative-news-update-and-the-week-in-review?e=1cb5fdae9e)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
https://www.goldentreewealth.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

ICYMI: our latest news and top stories from this week. Sign up today (https://www.illinoisreview.com/switching-plans-wizard?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) so that you never miss our breaking news!

BREAKING: IL Democratic State Senator Introduces Bill to Allow Illegal Aliens to Vote in School Board Elections (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/breaking-il-democratic-state-senator-introduces-bill-to-allow-illegal-aliens-to-vote-in-school-board-elections.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Earlier this week, Illinois Democratic State Sen. Celina Villanueva, Dist. 12, introduced legislation to allow “non-citizens of the United States” to register to vote in school board elections. The Democrats maintain a comfortable supermajority in the state Senate, 40-19.

The legislation was referred to the Assignments Committee where it will be reviewed – and if the committee decides to proceed with the bill, it will then be assigned to a specific committee.

The Democrats have a 4-2 majority on the Assignment Committee and the committee is chaired by Senate Majority Leader Kimberly Lightford, D.

Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/breaking-il-democratic-state-senator-introduces-bill-to-allow-illegal-aliens-to-vote-in-school-board-elections.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/breaking-il-democratic-state-senator-introduces-bill-to-allow-illegal-aliens-to-vote-in-school-board-elections.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

IL GOP Chair Don Tracy's Family-Owned Business Promoted #WhyIMask Face Mask Campaign During Pandemic (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/il-gop-chair-don-tracys-family-owned-business-promoted-whyimask-face-mask-campaign-during-pandemic.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Here in Illinois, Dot Foods, Inc. – a family-owned company that employs more than 6,000 people in the US and Canada – is the nation’s largest distributor of food and related products. Headquartered in Mt. Sterling, IL, the company is owned by twelve siblings, including IL GOP chairman Don Tracy, who is very proud of the business and mentions it frequently in speeches and interviews.

In December, Illinois Review reported that during the pandemic, owners of Dot Foods made a $6,193 donation to Democratic presidential candidate Joe Biden.

And just recently, Illinois Review has learned that at the same time Dot Foods owners were donating to Biden in 2020, they were simultaneously launching a #WhyIMask campaign, and encouraging employees to submit photos wearing a mask.
Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/il-gop-chair-don-tracys-family-owned-business-promoted-whyimask-face-mask-campaign-during-pandemic.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/il-gop-chair-don-tracys-family-owned-business-promoted-whyimask-face-mask-campaign-during-pandemic.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

The Real State of the Union (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/opinion-the-real-state-of-our-union.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
The Constitution requires that a president deliver a State of the Union address to Congress once a year.

Since we have a fraudulent occupant in the Oval Office at the moment, it is perhaps not surprising that his take on the State of the Union is equally false.

The actual state of the union, today, is much darker, much more vulnerable, than most people want to admit. And in our hearts, we all know it.

What is the state of the union today, in 2023?
Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/opinion-the-real-state-of-our-union.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/opinion-the-real-state-of-our-union.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Egofske Will Serve as Mayor-State Rep: Durkin, Who Voted for Weapons Ban Calls Him "Outstanding Public Servant" (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/egofske-will-serve-as-mayor-state-rep-durkin-who-voted-for-weapons-ban-calls-him-outstanding-public-servant.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
On Sunday, Lemont Mayor John Egofske was sworn in as the new Illinois State Representative for the 82nd District, replacing the vacancy left by former House Republican Minority leader Jim Durkin, who resigned his seat in early January after a string of embarrassing losses during the midterm election.

In a press release on Sunday, Durkin called his friend, whom he supported in a letter to the selection committee, an “outstanding public servant.”

Egofske, who is listed as an Independent on the Illinois State Board of Elections website, endorsed Illinois Democrat US Rep. Daniel Lipinski in the 2020 Primary and Illinois Democratic State Rep. Larry Walsh, Jr. during the 2022 general election.

Members of the selection committee tasked with finding Durkin’s replacement have said privately that Egofske pulled a Democratic Primary ballot in 2020 to vote for Lipinski.

During that same time, grassroots conservative Republican Mike Fricilone was on the 2020 Primary ballot running against two other Republican challengers – but Egofske chose to support the Democrat instead.
Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/egofske-will-serve-as-mayor-state-rep-durkin-who-voted-for-weapons-ban-calls-him-outstanding-public-servant.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/egofske-will-serve-as-mayor-state-rep-durkin-who-voted-for-weapons-ban-calls-him-outstanding-public-servant.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

BREAKING: Local Republican Leaders Select Lemont Mayor John Egofske, an Independent Who Endorsed Democrats, to Fill Jim Durkin's Vacant House Seat (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/breaking-local-republican-leaders-select-lemont-mayor-john-egofske-an-independent-who-endorsed-democrats-to-fill-jim-durkins-vacant-house-seat.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

Local Illinois Republican leaders from the 82nd House District have selected Lemont Mayor John Egofske to fill the vacant House seat left by former House Republican Minority Leader Jim Durkin, who resigned and left office in early January.

Egofske was elected mayor of Lemont in 2017, and his party affiliation on the Illinois State Board of Elections website is listed as an Independent.

In 2020, Egofske endorsed Democratic US Rep. Daniel Lipinski and on October 22, 2022, just weeks before the midterm election, Egofske endorsed Illinois Democratic State Rep. Larry Walsh, Jr., a member of Democratic House Speaker Chris Welch’s leadership team – where he serves as Downstate Caucus Whip, saying,

“Regardless of partisan politics, it’s all about getting things done, and I have seen first hand that Larry Walsh Jr. gets things done.” Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/breaking-local-republican-leaders-select-lemont-mayor-john-egofske-an-independent-who-endorsed-democrats-to-fill-jim-durkins-vacant-house-seat.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/breaking-local-republican-leaders-select-lemont-mayor-john-egofske-an-independent-who-endorsed-democrats-to-fill-jim-durkins-vacant-house-seat.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Chicago GOP Declares War on Free Speech, Silences Conservative Voices and Bans Illinois Review Articles on Facebook Discussion Group (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/chicago-gop-declares-war-on-free-speech-silences-conservative-voices-and-bans-illinois-review-articles-on-facebook-discussion-group.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

In the wake of DirectTV and AT&T censoring Newsmax and dropping the 4th highest rated conservative cable news channel in the country, a Chicago GOP Facebook discussion group run by Chicago Republican Party Director of Communications Jeff Fiedler, has decided to silence conservative voices, news, opinion and perspectives, and remove articles published by Illinois Review from their Facebook group. Click to read more. (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/02/chicago-gop-declares-war-on-free-speech-silences-conservative-voices-and-bans-illinois-review-articles-on-facebook-discussion-group.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

https://youtu.be/ATMgE9gv04c?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=6b35da287e-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_02_10_06_56&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-6b35da287e-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
Illinois Review Editor-in-Chief Mark Vargas appeared on The Stone Zone with Roger Stone to give an America First response to President Biden's State of the Union address.

body:

|
[**Survey: Do you trust Democrats to tell the truth -> **](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=ba56b573fd3741d59150bd4bc7fd425c&_e=qA25Nn3C9rPaxJm1UJuLbcbRSWZ-GKA204PXYFKBMkfp9dxi0isyM1Na2EkDnPTM5i1qzZl9yedSuoYuMsYF6gETU2BBwSa4jMHCbyuZMeYiAb1p5OtD8F2vOlLdCokmrX78J35m-QjThwb9zZGY89JLWNC_o_jA8DAbN2is7dV8yYktENUCauEGKX3JhdTIcPsOgTuJHJtcbVdU67jUgpHQxOFM7sn-Ru7aaen6cQ0clHUERVWx4BXflqa-fKLUboHPPGRqj5vgeRkCImkGFG4hX9pP6JfZq2rwD0BSANYuqNU-1j6x-jKey4xct4EsrLYST5rrE4TXOH0VfTx7mFQu8taRnPcqY_OJL3XAUwmRg6vkQh26TfWA6D5DbOncevJuKgZeguVrT0cqR7t-F8cpu2EOi-xE3zUfUHnNPxzCzk0syNkPS5h7-XSs8Z3CQE8hXbmfKd98xzFRQXkF9BY9GHIkYhV-_I3IwRqWgnY%3D)
Fellow Conservative,

President Biden’s speech to Congress was disappointing, dangerous, and at times, downright _bizarre_.

I hate to break the bad news to him, but 70% of Americans believe our country is headed in the wrong direction. They believe the economy is weak, that energy prices are too high, and the federal government wastes the hard-earned tax money they send to Washington.

They believe Biden’s open border has contributed to a dangerous increase in crime, and that for the past two years, America has projected an image of weakness to the world when we should be projecting strength and leadership.

Contrast that reality with what we heard in Biden’s speech, where he had the nerve to say the economy was booming, the border was secure, and he was standing up to China.

Really? Does he define “standing up to China” as letting a Chinese spy balloon hover over American military assets for only a week, instead of a month?

If Biden and the Democrats had a record to run on, they would! But they don’t, so instead they dodge, distract, and deceive...and hope the mainstream media lets them get away with it.

What you won’t hear from the mainstream media are the crazy policies they DO support, like:

* letting biological boys compete against girls in sports
* believing men can get pregnant
* open borders, and
* sending free money to able-bodied people who don’t work, while defunding the police who put their lives on the line every day to protect us

They’re hiding their real agenda, but I’m glad to say our House Republican majority is stepping up to stop it.

**[What I’m very curious to know – and why I’m emailing you today – is to find out whether you trust the government. More pointedly, do you trust Democrats to be truthful with you?](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=ba56b573fd3741d59150bd4bc7fd425c&_e=qA25Nn3C9rPaxJm1UJuLbcbRSWZ-GKA204PXYFKBMkfp9dxi0isyM1Na2EkDnPTM5i1qzZl9yedSuoYuMsYF6gETU2BBwSa4jMHCbyuZMeYiAb1p5OtD8F2vOlLdCokmrX78J35m-QjThwb9zZGY89JLWNC_o_jA8DAbN2is7dV8yYktENUCauEGKX3JhdTIcPsOgTuJHJtcbVdU67jUgpHQxOFM7sn-Ru7aaen6cQ0clHUERVWx4BXflqa-fKLUboHPPGRqj5vgeRkCImkGFG4hX9pP6JfZq2rwD0BSANYuqNU-1j6x-jKey4xct4EsKz_k0KC8dTRaXcFgzceL4LpybFrj-pKH73sD3fpdKmTAH2GY61NN2kojM9HD7b0t8KKJE_XwRvSud0TXwaIse0CDjvFOTP_pjVdk11UQD6moR4B4KLPEN4Kglj8H8CdLbkEv7lnYMUtNQVGmvZ8P3m6hUEaez8NXy3eIk2Coy9c%3D)**

**About Jim Jordan:** | Leading the fight to hold Washington accountable.

**
In Washington, Jim Jordan is focused on his oversight role, helping lead investigations into federal agencies

In Congress, Jim Jordan continued to fight for fiscal responsibility,and he offered the only balanced budget of any member of the House or Senate.

As a state legislator, he took on the powerful special interests and the leaders from both political parties who wanted to put Ohio on an unsustainable path of higher taxes and spending.

He built a conservative, pro-family voting record, and authored an alternative state budget that balanced without raising taxes. He became the only legislator in state history to win both the Defender of Life award from Ohio Right to Life and the Pro-Life Legislator of the Year award from the United Conservatives of Ohio.

clean_body: President Biden’s speech to Congress was disappointing, dangerous, and at times, downright bizarre. | | Fellow Conservative, President Biden’s speech to Congress was disappointing, dangerous, and at times, downright bizarre. I hate to break the bad news to him, but 70% of Americans believe our country is headed in the wrong direction. They believe the economy is weak, that energy prices are too high, and the federal government wastes the hard-earned tax money they send to Washington. They believe Biden’s open border has contributed to a dangerous increase in crime, and that for the past two years, America has projected an image of weakness to the world when we should be projecting strength and leadership. Contrast that reality with what we heard in Biden’s speech, where he had the nerve to say the economy was booming, the border was secure, and he was standing up to China. Really? Does he define “standing up to China” as letting a Chinese spy balloon hover over American military assets for only a week, instead of a month? If Biden and the Democrats had a record to run on, they would! But they don’t, so instead they dodge, distract, and deceive...and hope the mainstream media lets them get away with it. What you won’t hear from the mainstream media are the crazy policies they DO support, like: letting biological boys compete against girls in sports believing men can get pregnant open borders, and sending free money to able-bodied people who don’t work, while defunding the police who put their lives on the line every day to protect us They’re hiding their real agenda, but I’m glad to say our House Republican majority is stepping up to stop it. I’m worried that we could be losing support for our movement. My team tells me that folks aren’t opening our emails as often as they once did or even engaging with the content we send. I implore you to remain involved. God Bless, Jim Jordan About Jim Jordan: | Leading the fight to hold Washington accountable. In Washington, Jim Jordan is focused on his oversight role, helping lead investigations into federal agencies In Congress, Jim Jordan continued to fight for fiscal responsibility,and he offered the only balanced budget of any member of the House or Senate. He built a conservative, pro-family voting record, and authored an alternative state budget that balanced without raising taxes. He became the only legislator in state history to win both the Defender of Life award from Ohio Right to Life and the Pro-Life Legislator of the Year award from the United Conservatives of Ohio. | Paid for by Jim Jordan for Congress PO BOX 355 Delaware, OH 43015

name: OcasioCortez.com
subject: Alexandria’s time
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Alexandria does things differently.
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20230210-3878-header&t=1&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)
Unlike most other politicians, Alexandria doesn’t spend her time schmoozing for donations from big donors over the phone or at exclusive events. All of her time and energy is dedicated to serving her community and working people across the country.
We instead fundraise through online donations from supporters who give $5 or $10 at a time through emails like these, so Alexandria can stay focused on fighting for bold legislation, organizing our communities, and standing up against powerful special interests.
But to be totally honest, our fundraising isn’t where it needs to be in order for us to reach our fundraising targets. So to fill the gap, **it’s critical that we ramp up fundraising in the next month to hit our fundraising goal for this quarter.**
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230210-2.maq8uR&refcode=em20230210-3878-P_FND_EGR_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)
**If at least every one in three people who open this email chips in, we’ll make up our lost ground in no time.** But we know not everyone is in the place to give today — and we completely understand.
[If you’re able to pitch in today, it would help guarantee we have the resources to back Alexandria’s goals and keep her in Congress fighting for working families. Can you contribute any amount right now to power our campaign?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230210-2.maq8uR&refcode=em20230210-3878-P_FND_EGR_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230210-2.maq8uR&refcode=em20230210-3878-P_FND_EGR_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)**
Our work is only possible because of the support and dedication from individuals like you who believe in Alexandria’s vision of a more equitable future for all.
Thank you,
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230210-2?refcode=em20230210-3878-P_FND_EGR_GFM&t=23&refcode2=3878_2526796_8xixSd&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230210-3878-P_FND_EGR_GFM&t=24&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=25&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=26&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=27&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=28&akid=3878%2E2526796%2E8xixSd)

| I’ll be honest, Peter: we really beat the odds in the 2022 midterm elections. Even the most optimistic pundits called our race a TOSS-UP right up until Election Day. But even with the odds stacked against us, a self-funding millionaire opponent, and national Republicans spending big in their efforts to unseat me, **I defended my seat thanks to supporters like you.**

It has consistently been shown that early money is the best way to win a campaign. Our people-powered campaign last cycle was proof of that – it was because of early investments from grassroots donors like you that we were able to start voter outreach early and continue our momentum all the way through Election Day.

I know I can defend my seat again in 2024 and continue advocating for Pennsylvanians on the issues that matter most, but we’re going to need an even stronger foundation this time. **I’m asking you today to chip in****$10****or whatever makes sense for you towards building that foundation.[_Anything you can spare will make a real difference for our campaign, not just today but all the way through to the next Election Day._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58550398/391468348/-1109429433?refcode=BLU-SWC-20230210-1-EM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODczMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODEzNWIyYS03ZmE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ty-4Bl05lD1k-qD9QNsiBUEJHZzHQbVCc7AmwxHvnCc=)**

subject: If McCarthy won’t act, we will
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Every second that George Santos remains in office is a threat to our democracy, and McCarthy’s futile attempts to change the conversation are nothing but a distraction. |
---
_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _

name: Working Families Party
subject: Join our call with Randi Weingarten, Brandon Johnson, Helen Gym and more
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St
body:
Building WFP power from the bottom up | | | Peter, Many might think 2023 is an off-election year, but here at the Working Families Party, we know that every year is an election year. And municipal races in cities like Philadelphia and Chicago in 2023 give us a unique opportunity to build WFP governing power from the bottom up. That’s why we are excited to join up with the American Federation of Teachers and throw our support behind two champions —**Brandon Johnson in Chicago and Helen Gym in Philadelphia** — two former public school teachers who are running for mayor to ensure their respective cities put working people first. **Get to know Brandon Johnson and Helen Gym and their progressive vision for their communities this Monday at a briefing call hosted by WFP National Director Maurice Mitchell and AFT President Randi Weingarten.[RSVP to join the virtual call this Monday, February 13 at 3:30pm ET/2:30pm CT. That’s why AFT and WFP are joining forces for Brandon and Helen — two progressive champions and public school teachers who have what it takes to build multiracial, working-class power and make a meaningful difference in their cities. clean_body: Building WFP power from the bottom up | | | Peter, Many might think 2023 is an off-election year, but here at the Working Families Party, we know that every year is an election year. And municipal races in cities like Philadelphia and Chicago in 2023 give us a unique opportunity to build WFP governing power from the bottom up. That’s why we are excited to join up with the American Federation of Teachers and throw our support behind two champions —Brandon Johnson in Chicago and Helen Gym in Philadelphia — two former public school teachers who are running for mayor to ensure their respective cities put working people first. Get to know Brandon Johnson and Helen Gym and their progressive vision for their communities this Monday at a briefing call hosted by WFP National Director Maurice Mitchell and AFT President Randi Weingarten. We know that when we invest in municipal races like mayor and city council seats, we have the opportunity to grow our party and our movement. City races are where we’ve already elected some of the most crucial and visible WFP champions, from Kendra Brooks in Philadelphia to Candi Cdebaca in Denver, which in turn creates the best opportunities to recruit more working class people, especially working class Black and brown folks, into our voter bloc. That’s why AFT and WFP are joining forces for Brandon and Helen — two progressive champions and public school teachers who have what it takes to build multiracial, working-class power and make a meaningful difference in their cities. During Monday’s briefing, we’ll hear from both Brandon and Helen, and get an insider’s view on the strategy and analysis surrounding these two critical races. Hope to see you there, Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . _Join us this Sunday to celebrate our community of volunteers!_ We are living in a moment that calls for hope, light and love. Hope for our futures, light to see our way forward, and love for one another. Our community of volunteers provide all three. This **Sunday, Feb 12th from 6pm-9pm** 7 Directions of Service is hosting a **Volunteer Appreciation PROM** where we will recognize the impact and power of the volunteers who drive our liberatory work forward. We have exciting developments to share, and we'll be celebrating Jason Crazy Bear’s birthday too! **** We'll kick off our Volunteer Appreciation at 6pm with a vegetarian-friendly New Orleans-inspired dinner by Chef Taylor of [By The Brook Ferments](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/SVVf6UGddUySPN6znLYVWgilExxaoQb13SrRD5tglydj-PreEfww2qxNeHVWNKpcDh_PfmnxFk4Ku-_hNVedkK_TGoapCMWgoStarkWFJYHlu9aZnUhV9OaZAlKzoRKx1NOoWIEu7MirEZ5jP_BpLF78OiYT1N9P3TxOUN6F5Z5NcwZheuXTtUMP3_LzNHCVbLOVACors9HP8-7TSAenD2A464Gnag0VIWCiz-a37xeyzCuIStVH_PSsraMr20PU3bByLtzGbJdGllb3m68Prw/3tl/-XpQ997HSwG8gJ2b02Jdxw/h1/FP5rOevaCjm5AJ6ITU3aZhICyTZ95Cmg592297RIt9k) All are welcome!

| Submit a comment to the US Forest Service! **** We have another chance to tell the USFS that the Mountain Valley Pipeline's attempt to cut and blast across the Jefferson National Forest must be rejected. Construction would further pollute the waters of West Virginia and Virginia, destroy vital forest habitats, and harm communities. **** The US Forest Service (USFS) has opened a comment period on their new Draft Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement after their old permits for MVP were twice denied in court.

| Submit a comment to the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers! **** The company behind MVP has submitted additional information to their Clean Water Act Section 404 permit application to cross underneath hundreds of streams and rivers, under the jurisdiction of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. MVP’s new information only reinforces that the pipeline cannot be built without lasting, irreversible damage to the waters in its path, to aquatic life, to our communities and to the climate. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers needs to hear from you that MVP is not in the public interest! | Our awesome coalition partners at [POWHR](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/1XQbiUZqIgkFRQxQopJGKdCPNchRQqm-AbJSVmdV9FtpLBEwp8hPkAjBzLtfrF7xU_jeMjFxVbee-JwQX-GHvCTHb3-6_uzLiX3IVu8mxColAWGt7_qr6sNEEC3yZptJauu_WJ6lfR-O_b6M9TAoZMw1s2m2fc1hDMekpSsWob8sGwxIU_P6NZ9OsV4PTl-h87I_bZCCvVkTN8Odr35D5vbSt_S6WK5rNFe4wSk0rYzQPLMv9MBkk6td0xvQ2e0E/3tl/-XpQ997HSwG8gJ2b02Jdxw/h21/vBw4s25La2aOyTfJ5UplE77yBbYknnD5YwAFdskzwEU) are hosting a teach-in on the movement to [Stop Cop City](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDCcI0D0aBjT5PO7At0OyHLSxiG-pJmAsiTC6G4NW_ibouDVI-nM0FFnE5Ft8mZCvkoEcyYPzqmjHpdKuP3PL5rwntLlQtjoRCRdNTyh7I-JI0XLMAc6JKBj3J24pHvNU9QXfaVZXarP3D7MzJ5AIZUTLmeCdr9e9FazTgerldT_z1EmizAi0UgqNFAZMuZVl1icTL_W7PKC4mZm0d2JGrae41oUH0Uk-xNcT6rkP2ZCs6SGqWpet4gb9XYuJW6EWw9y76ZV5KazghZxhtP9V9BY/3tl/-XpQ997HSwG8gJ2b02Jdxw/h22/9tggA2-TX78JYGu9KjFIZ7LOQnB1fJmp15qrq5-z5sU) and how it relates to our fight to stop the Mountain Valley Pipeline. The Atlanta Police Foundation, corporations, and local officials are trying to destroy hundreds of acres of the Weelaunee Forest to build a massive police training compound. The compound would train cops from around the world in militarized police tactics. On January 18th, police murdered forest defender Tortuguita who had been organizing tirelessly to #StopCopCity. Cop City must be stopped. This teach-in is for the movement to stop the Mountain Valley Pipeline to learn about the connections between our two movements and how we can support #StopCopCity. Speakers include: ★ Denali Nalamalapu on how #StopCopCity connects to #StopMVP ★ Emily Backus on organizing with #StopCopCity on the ground in Atlanta ★ Crystal Mello on building relationships amongst grassroots environmental justice fights ★ Russell Chisholm on escalated police presence and the criminalization of protests in the US ★ Basav Sen on murders of environmental defenders around the world **Register here :** [https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/registertZUpfu2gqTItHtDjPet18sWHiY5OY2DYiGSm](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/1XQbiUZqIgkFRQxQopJGKaUQptCMOORsRtvmT5k6_Hr0UN5JChjEj4rmpgzbkTCf7BY1NL8GOooLbRA70YBscfyY9hav0PUWVEzzlQU2g7p-6Ncy2RIbBkiVtywU-GMHniNl6WkUFcF_Xvfblm1nIeRZ8SacuJeFPn24jah66Id_l4TbjKk5J-7xXdJfcQI2cuAw748D2H14iyouyWuBxVgY6-p-mTBHzQzC03fpKMlcxJicYj2qs12t621fqJ_ESQ6Te_SR7TYtoZBUMcVoOHE2YTgNX2jruthtg2PBd6_Bv0kPCoYhnHbl24-xl3x_weCq568Qwr-dlcX9ehcj0A367MNy1leJHxnyWCs9SSA/3tl/-XpQ997HSwG8gJ2b02Jdxw/h23/9phjtq6VNUVlfG8QTVVqy6_Akb2OFDuZK6wycQofAl4)

clean_body: Join us this Sunday for Volunteer Appreciation PROM, and meet our new team members! | | Join us this Sunday to celebrate our community of volunteers! We are living in a moment that calls for hope, light and love. Hope for our futures, light to see our way forward, and love for one another. Our community of volunteers provide all three. This Sunday, Feb 12th from 6pm-9pm 7 Directions of Service is hosting a Volunteer Appreciation PROM where we will recognize the impact and power of the volunteers who drive our liberatory work forward. We have exciting developments to share, and we'll be celebrating Jason Crazy Bear’s birthday too! ** We'll kick off our Volunteer Appreciation at 6pm with a vegetarian-friendly New Orleans-inspired dinner by Chef Taylor of All are welcome! | | | | | Submit a comment to the US Forest Service! We have another chance to tell the USFS that the Mountain Valley Pipeline's attempt to cut and blast across the Jefferson National Forest must be rejected. Construction would further pollute the waters of West Virginia and Virginia, destroy vital forest habitats, and harm communities. The US Forest Service (USFS) has opened a comment period on their new Draft Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement after their old permits for MVP were twice denied in court. View a recording of the latest and the Draft your own comment using this and these Use this from Wild Virginia Check out the recording of West Virginia Rivers' lunch and learn here and use their action alert: | Submit a comment to the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers! ** The company behind MVP has submitted additional information to their Clean Water Act Section 404 permit application to cross underneath hundreds of streams and rivers, under the jurisdiction of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. MVP’s new information only reinforces that the pipeline cannot be built without lasting, irreversible damage to the waters in its path, to aquatic life, to our communities and to the climate. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers needs to hear from you that MVP is not in the public interest! The risks to our waters, communities and future outweigh any supposed benefits this project could provide. Submit a quick comment letter to the US Forest Service with Write your own comment to the US Army Corps of Engineers by February 25th using and View West Virginia Rivers' comment party on BOTH comment periods, recording here: Use West Virginia Rivers’ easy action alerts to submit your comments: a quick comment letter to the US Forest Service with Appalachian Voices Don't miss this! | | Our awesome coalition partners at are hosting a teach-in on the movement to and how it relates to our fight to stop the Mountain Valley Pipeline. The Atlanta Police Foundation, corporations, and local officials are trying to destroy hundreds of acres of the Weelaunee Forest to build a massive police training compound. The compound would train cops from around the world in militarized police tactics. On January 18th, police murdered forest defender Tortuguita who had been organizing tirelessly to #StopCopCity. Cop City must be stopped. This teach-in is for the movement to stop the Mountain Valley Pipeline to learn about the connections between our two movements and how we can support #StopCopCity. Speakers include: ★ Denali Nalamalapu on how #StopCopCity connects to #StopMVP ★ Emily Backus on organizing with #StopCopCity on the ground in Atlanta ★ Crystal Mello on building relationships amongst grassroots environmental justice fights ★ Russell Chisholm on escalated police presence and the criminalization of protests in the US ★ Basav Sen on murders of environmental defenders around the world Register here : Bila:huk, 7 Directions of Service | | | Sent via . | Team,

As the Arizona Democratic Party, we have a pretty simple mission at the core of our work – **_we elect Democrats to office._**

And for the last several election cycles, we’ve had a lot of success. Arizona voters are increasingly **electing Democrats** to local, state, and federal offices. **_So when people ask what our plan for 2024 is, it’s pretty simple: continue to elect Democrats to every seat on the ballot!_**

Arizona voters have picked Democrats to represent them completely in the U.S. Senate, and they have for several election cycles. **From now until November 2024, our plan is to make sure a Democrat is once again elected to represent Arizona in the U.S. Senate.**

**[Team, our core mission has been set for decades, and it’s not going to change now. | #### [ Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders Delivered This GOP State of the Union Rebuttal Tonight after President Biden gave his mandatory State of the Union address to the nation, two Republicans gave rebuttals. Sarah Huckabee Sanders, Arkansas’s newly elected Governor gave her rebuttal directly after Biden finished his speech. In her speech, the Governor was predicted early on after being tapped for the rebuttal that she would focus on age and refreshing the GOP. Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week.
**** INDems Announces Impressive Group of Candidates for 2023 Municipal Races This week, INDems was proud to announce some of the talented and diverse candidates who are running for mayor in cities across our state. Each of these individuals bring a wealth of experience and fresh perspectives to their races and are committed to working for the betterment of their communities. ******[Share Hamilton County Reporter](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552613/391470037/-589223693?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535)** Here are a few candidates that are running to make gains in mayoral offices across Indiana: In Carmel, Miles Nelson, a small business owner and the first Democratic city councilor in city history, is running for mayor in the open seat left by the retirement of longtime Mayor Jim Brainard. Democrats have made major inroads in the Indianapolis suburb with [Democratic candidates winning the city](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552616/391470039/-162848712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) in 2022 and [President Joe Biden winning](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552618/391470041/-162848712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) the “roundabout capital of the world" in 2020. In Evansville, [Stephanie Terry](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552620/391470043/590650319?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535), a current county councilor and executive director of the Children’s Museum of Evansville, announced her campaign for mayor. Incumbent Republican Lloyd Winnecke announced he would not seek a fourth term and [has already endorsed](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552621/391470046/-1576762404?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) a candidate in what will likely be a contentious primary for the Republican nomination. In West Lafayette, West Lafayette Development Director Erin Easter is running for mayor. At her announcement, Easter was [endorsed by current West Lafayette mayor John Dennis](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552622/391470047/-1163448650?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535), a Republican. In Muncie, City Council President Jeff Robinson announced he is [running against one-term incumbent](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552632/391470048/-898492449?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) Dan Ridenour. Robinson announced that his slogan would be “Putting Muncie First” and has worked with both Republicans and Democrats while on the council – even picking up [Republican votes](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552634/391470049/-1081884217?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) when he was elected Council President. See INDems’ full release by [clicking here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552635/391470050/608382556?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535). ****_President Biden Delivers Fact after Fact during State of the Union_ ******[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552636/391470051/648398157?s=20&t=3tlqeBY4MuAHnCHbZLWBhg&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535)** President Biden gave his annual State of the Union address this week and showed that the state of the union is strong and getting stronger. The president affirmed that his plan to build the economy from the bottom up and middle out is working: Unemployment is at record lows, the economy is growing and inflation is going down. **President Biden and INDems are ready and working to deliver a better future for all Hoosiers. We can do more by electing Democrats up-and-down the ballot in 2023 and 2024 to give Indiana some common sense leadership.** **** Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. ******[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552637/391470052/1463954024?s=20&t=3tlqeBY4MuAHnCHbZLWBhg&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535)** State Senator Shelli Yoder passed a bill out of the State Senate unanimously this week. The proposal would [expand access to long-acting contraception](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552638/391470053/1635770283?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) for Hoosiers. Sen. Yoder said the following after its passage, “This is a win-win for Hoosiers because it saves money for our health care providers and it’s a convenience for our Medicaid recipients.” [SB 252](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58552639/391470054/-450072652?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZGU1OWM0MC04MWE5LWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5ByhL2LMQtZjVmJ9vglTKh4V3pHG_a--XmiV5GMA9mA=&emci=7ac46965-6ca9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&emdi=8de59c40-81a9-ed11-994d-00224832eb73&ceid=4925535) now heads to the House of Representatives to be considered. **Opportunities with INDems** Operations Coordinator **T** The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Operations Coordinator to help and support our team. In working with the Chair and Executive Director, the Coordinator will oversee the office, run payroll and oversee benefits, oversee IT and office equipment, accounts, and files. They will also be involved in event planning and execution and ensure that the party and its staff and projects all run smoothly. **O** Overall Duties:
* Oversees operations of the office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces. Manages the relationships with building and parking companies.
* Works on overall organization of filing both physical and digital (recordkeeping) and ensures best practices for IT and security.
* Oversees phone system, internet, printer, and supplies to ensure proper functioning.
* Responsible for onboarding of all new staff and helps current staff navigate any issues with pay, benefits, schedules, etc.
* Point person for health benefits, 401(k), insurance, payroll administrator, reimbursements, travel / logistics, etc.
* Takes the lead on event planning and execution in partnership with the Executive Director and other team members:
* Tours and reviews spaces for events.
* Works with caterers, vendors, and sound and stage consultants.
* Responsible for successful operations with annual dinner, biennial conventions, party retreat, annual holiday party, and other party functions.
* Responsible for establishing coordinated field offices, including securing space, obtaining proper liability insurance, and utilities.

Qualifications: Minimum of at least two (2) years with campaign experience or similar. Experience working in operations or customer service is also preferred.
* Good verbal, written, and analytical skills.
* Experience following and updating budgets.
* Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time.
* Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment.
* Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism.
* Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality.

Salary: Salary is $50,000 per annum. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Position is part of the staff union. Application Instructions: Please email your resume and three professional references to jobs@indems.org with “Operations Coordinator” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP.
* * *
## **Executive Director**
The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Executive Director (ED) to lead our team. In partnership with the Chair and State Central Committee, the ED is responsible for deploying the strategy, overall management, and growth of IDP. The ED will be involved in all aspects of the Party’s operations, including but not limited to: election activities, voter protection, fundraising, budgeting, staff recruitment and retention, outreach, and building party infrastructure. General Duties:** **
* Responsible for overall operational efficiency of IDP, including staff and creation and execution of Party strategy.
* Recruits, interviews, hires, and manages IDP and coordinated campaign staff.
* Manages day-to-day work of Communications, Data & Organizing, Operations, and Finance teams.
* Oversees the day-to-day work of outside consultants and vendors.
* Serves as one of the spokespeople for IDP to the media.
* Other responsibilities as required.

Party Affairs
* Coordinates work and materials for the Democratic State Central Committee, District leaders, County leaders, and precinct committeemen and women.
* Keeps up strong relationships with various constituencies and stakeholders around the state, including organized labor, African-American leaders, Latino leaders, LGBTQ Hoosiers, women’s groups, Indiana Young Democrats, and College Democrats.
* Maintains close working relationships with various party partner organizations including, but not limited to: DNC, ASDC, DCCC, DSCC, DGA, and DLCC.
* Builds and nurtures relationships with Democratic candidates, elected officials, Democratic caucuses, and staff members across the state.
* Supports the building, growth, and success of county parties throughout the state.
* Oversees an annual party retreat, annual fundraising dinner, biennial state conventions, and the National Delegate Selection process for the 2024 Democratic convention.

Campaign & Election Activities
* Shares regular organizing, training, coaching, and other resources online and in-person for Party staff, volunteers, and party leadership.
* Works with IDP and Indiana Election Division to ensure the Party is up-to-date on the current and latest rules relating to elections and voting in Indiana.
* Troubleshoots any issues with campaigns or county parties on behalf of IDP to ensure the Party is represented well and that issues are resolved in a timely fashion.** **
* Oversees voter protection in-state and in partnership with DNC guidance.
* Oversees GOTV and Election Day efforts and events for IDP.

Fundraising
* Works with the State Chair to raise money for the party through call time, meetings, events, memberships, online giving, and other income generating streams.
* Partners with the finance team to create a robust apparatus for fundraising and cultivating large and small donors.
* Represents IDP at party, media, or community functions across the state.

Operations & Compliance
* Manages office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces.
* Keeps an updated and working knowledge of campaign finance, election, and compliance laws and works in concert with the compliance team and legal counsel to ensure all Party activities are consistently compliant.
* Works with the State Chair and Party CPA and Treasurer to implement the budget.

Qualifications: Minimum of at least four (4) years with direct campaign experience. Executive experience working on a coordinated campaign, statewide or Congressional political campaign, or with a state political party preferred.
* Experience managing a growing, diverse, and highly skilled team, with demonstrated ability to achieve high levels of performance and outcomes through guidance and management.
* A reputation for leading with integrity and making decisions with the input of the appropriate stakeholders.
* Experience building, managing, and tracking large budgets.
* Excellent verbal, written, and analytical skills.
* Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time.
* Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment.
* Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism.
* Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality.

Salary:
Salary is generally competitive and commensurate with experience. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Application Instructions:
Please email your resume and three professional references to [jobs@indems.org](mailto:jobs@indems.org) with “Executive Director” in the subject line. Thank you for your interest in working with IDP.

clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party’s weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. ** INDems Announces Impressive Group of Candidates for 2023 Municipal Races This week, INDems was proud to announce some of the talented and diverse candidates who are running for mayor in cities across our state. Each of these individuals bring a wealth of experience and fresh perspectives to their races and are committed to working for the betterment of their communities. ** Here are a few candidates that are running to make gains in mayoral offices across Indiana: In Carmel, Miles Nelson, a small business owner and the first Democratic city councilor in city history, is running for mayor in the open seat left by the retirement of longtime Mayor Jim Brainard. Democrats have made major inroads in the Indianapolis suburb with in 2022 and the “roundabout capital of the world" in 2020. In Evansville, , a current county councilor and executive director of the Children’s Museum of Evansville, announced her campaign for mayor. Incumbent Republican Lloyd Winnecke announced he would not seek a fourth term and a candidate in what will likely be a contentious primary for the Republican nomination. In West Lafayette, West Lafayette Development Director Erin Easter is running for mayor. At her announcement, Easter was , a Republican. In Muncie, City Council President Jeff Robinson announced he is Dan Ridenour. Robinson announced that his slogan would be “Putting Muncie First” and has worked with both Republicans and Democrats while on the council – even picking up when he was elected Council President. See INDems’ full release by . **President Biden Delivers Fact after Fact during State of the Union ** President Biden gave his annual State of the Union address this week and showed that the state of the union is strong and getting stronger. The president affirmed that his plan to build the economy from the bottom up and middle out is working: Unemployment is at record lows, the economy is growing and inflation is going down. President Biden and INDems are ready and working to deliver a better future for all Hoosiers. We can do more by electing Democrats up-and-down the ballot in 2023 and 2024 to give Indiana some common sense leadership. ** Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. ** State Senator Shelli Yoder passed a bill out of the State Senate unanimously this week. The proposal would for Hoosiers. Sen. Yoder said the following after its passage, “This is a win-win for Hoosiers because it saves money for our health care providers and it’s a convenience for our Medicaid recipients.” now heads to the House of Representatives to be considered. **** **** NDTC & Indiana Democratic Party Blue Bench Training Join the National Democratic Training Committee & Indiana Democratic Party on Saturday, February 25th from 9-4 pm ET for a free, in-person Blue Bench training. If you’re running for office, working on a campaign, or working with your local Democratic Party, this training will provide the tools, skills, and insight you need to succeed. During this full-day training, you’ll: -Build the foundational skills needed to run a successful campaign or strengthen your community’s Democratic infrastructure. -Learn from campaign experts and gain key insight to guide your efforts in 2023. -Connect with leaders across Indiana to network, support, and learn from each other’s work. -Understand how to access on-going resources to support your goals. 2023 IDEA Convention: Save the Date! ****INDems IN the News : U.S. reports blowout job growth; unemployment lowest since 1969 : Bill aims to free Indiana schools from 'regulatory handcuffs.' Teachers worry it’ll silence them : Indiana residential property tax relief bill draws opposition from schools, local officials : Indiana lawmakers try to force hospitals to lower prices : New statewide poll shows most Indiana parents 'satisfied' with schools, classroom curricula : Local public school officials react to potential school choice expansion : Election skeptics slow to get sweeping changes in GOP states : Anti-ESG pension bill could drop state pension returns $6.7 billion in next decade : A push to legalize cannabis in Indiana continues to struggle for a foothold : Birth-control and housing bills pass chambers : Comcast investing in $50 million project to expand broadban across Indiana including in Porter and LaPorte counties : Indiana earned income tax credit increase unanimously clears House : Indiana Republicans back switch to partisan school board elections : Indiana bill would fund handgun training for teachers : Democrats putting up more mayoral candidates across Indiana : Indiana’s GOP lawmakers split on bill to offer ‘driving privilege’ cards to immigrants : House committee OKs bill seen as anti-transgender targeting DCS care Opportunities with INDems Operations Coordinator T The Indiana Democratic Party (IDP) is seeking an Operations Coordinator to help and support our team. In working with the Chair and Executive Director, the Coordinator will oversee the office, run payroll and oversee benefits, oversee IT and office equipment, accounts, and files. They will also be involved in event planning and execution and ensure that the party and its staff and projects all run smoothly. O Overall Duties: Oversees operations of the office and ensures campaigns and committees have productive workspaces. Manages the relationships with building and parking companies. Works on overall organization of filing both physical and digital (recordkeeping) and ensures best practices for IT and security. Oversees phone system, internet, printer, and supplies to ensure proper functioning. Responsible for onboarding of all new staff and helps current staff navigate any issues with pay, benefits, schedules, etc. Point person for health benefits, 401(k), insurance, payroll administrator, reimbursements, travel / logistics, etc. Takes the lead on event planning and execution in partnership with the Executive Director and other team members: Tours and reviews spaces for events. Works with caterers, vendors, and sound and stage consultants. Responsible for successful operations with annual dinner, biennial conventions, party retreat, annual holiday party, and other party functions. Responsible for establishing coordinated field offices, including securing space, obtaining proper liability insurance, and utilities. Qualifications: Minimum of at least two (2) years with campaign experience or similar. Experience working in operations or customer service is also preferred. Good verbal, written, and analytical skills. Experience following and updating budgets. Ability to manage several tasks and projects at the same time. Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment. Detail oriented and good follow through on tasks. Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact professionally with staff and external stakeholders and represent the organization with professionalism. Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality. Salary: Salary is $50,000 per annum. Position includes full medical, dental, and vision insurance benefits, a 401(k) account, and monthly tech stipend. Position is part of the staff union. Works with IDP and Indiana Election Division to ensure the Party is up-to-date on the current and latest rules relating to elections and voting in Indiana. Troubleshoots any issues with campaigns or county parties on behalf of IDP to ensure the Party is represented well and that issues are resolved in a timely fashion. Oversees voter protection in-state and in partnership with DNC guidance. Oversees GOTV and Election Day efforts and events for IDP. body:
| |
---

---
| | Dear Peter, My name is Harmeet Dhillon, the founder and Chief Executive Officer of the Center for American Liberty, and I'm asking for your immediate help to stop the radical Left's medical mutilation of our children in the name of "gender dysphoria."
---
| | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310078:JaU1DDGUN:m:1:3331421248:6598691A18AF57A8088D3F3E108F1F00:r)
---
| We were recently on Tucker Carlson Tonight, announcing that we just launched a landmark case against the hospitals AND doctors who coerced Chloe Cole to transition to a "boy" at the young age of 13.
---
| | **In case you missed our announcement on Tucker 's show, let me quickly give you the details of our case: ** As a young 13-year-old girl, doctors began transitioning Chloe to become a boy...
---
_This included putting her on experimental drugs like**Lupron** , a drug that has been used to chemically castrate violent sex offenders..._
---
_... Giving her constant testosterone injections..._ _... And removing both of her breasts in a double-mastectomy surgery at the age of 15._ **_How was this allowed to happen?_** It's because the doctors and hospitals lied to the family -- including one powerful lie constantly used to scare parents into agreeing to this medical mutilation:
---
"Would you rather have a dead daughter or a live son?"
---
This lie is repeated to struggling families so doctors and hospitals can perform lucrative transgender medical experimentation on children... And I'm asking for your help today to put an end to it! [**Specifically, I 'm asking you to make a one-time emergency donation of $25 today to hold the hospitals AND doctors responsible for their barbaric actions. **](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62310078:JaU1DDGUN:m:1:3331421248:6598691A18AF57A8088D3F3E108F1F00:r) Because until these predators are held responsible things will never change... And if we don't act right away, more and more children will be put under the knife and experimented on. Medical facilities are actively promoting, encouraging, and advertising these barbaric treatments to minors. Chloe has suffered physically, socially, emotionally, and psychologically from their lies and experiments. Chloe also has no idea if she'll ever experience the profound joy of motherhood... and if she is able to one day have kids, she knows she will not be able to breastfeed them. The medical experimentation performed on Chloe as a young child caused permanent and irreversible damage to her body and the predators that did this must be held accountable... ...and that's where you come in. I’ve said this before, and I will say it one trillion times more for the folks in the back—Twitter—or any platform—doesn’t work if people can’t have free speech. And it’s not free speech if you’re not allowed to see it, like it, retweet it, and reply to it. From the jump, Big Tech has worked double overtime to [stifle our voices.](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2f80561774f943d9b514d0549ec6f868&_e=wNR48WDyK11PYFpJqAkW5nJ7gA5E9VvYcyj9HyYtwCosjsvcJoTKmF4Evu2oJNd_Nxr77Dmz-QFIR_l8cfum7-orrp0TJTGG9DGZB4T6rJdYTIUaScjoZXGxpAUdgnJVJWzAOXga_2vkThinBetjNxrNytCcuUiOFk3Az4F_MDUBR4kp_FJYyzKvXJkG4osSCrTRiJJZrVoK-_DrLMmDz4kqNmBBa2vILiUsJHbaNrDZ0SnBsmfkFG7oC3SUNMx8ioNcGHOg8Iv1sesmYFsT0dVFwCQ5ifYWBix5rEVLduo%3D) Mind you, the Twitter bureaucrats had no problem letting the [ayatollahs in Iran](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2f80561774f943d9b514d0549ec6f868&_e=wNR48WDyK11PYFpJqAkW5kYzUcLyiGtzDMPOCTfdYl4tMPUS212T4uPYRsu1dawk1pC9TdczzS9Ttpq2W7BDGG9MY5UI1k_8tMJ3rBYOVt7O2rYA-A-sfEHZC3GU1lKZ_R8oEu1HpedVutZqZjKf2vFhqguVIBNiw2eMpJxeFblwB4yl85C2obYTeMa44FOTpIKu1hS9WTa--fUE1cCplD0Jhb0_efvFJ0SRjQ6ZYgiuPPLPvcG-tO1p_ZHW8ENPMgcSC6aTW6kh54zrFYQ9Y99BQ__9sR1MH9DTVPYO-6qIViphIe7PjZtT3m8iIKVJyP-jkXwmc2MPixHt1_zdoGYOvpidL9ngsqujODlsqgk9G2CDCVPKDhfVEU2dBKVnW2HA-pEj4IEAJ9VMWz1Xhg%3D%3D), the [communists in China](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2f80561774f943d9b514d0549ec6f868&_e=wNR48WDyK11PYFpJqAkW5osPfqEijikF0Qo-SCNclphSgKZKB6sxzTr8HGo-2zZCdsCesPthcsx8_NeypxwpGuDORETp0AWg9x39g1MgXuhGJlA58EQ3DeyBdh4IUrSlH5NgW_k6y12LmbCBXi7t9jDu_1GeUVja2jDdT2dMAQ05JjI5HKrr_gk1H6C3U_k7bU_gQmTFG9xqz087RWCzTvFjzDbFvdX8ROhvt8jZXNIWBRsNqNGYKWq9dCK_WhRe0RoMNl_dZL4HjiWzvu_5m9HFHMyk1ksP0EBjzM0Wiz4%3D), and various other terrorist dirtbags say whatever they darn well pleased on the platform. Newsflash for the constitution haters out there—free speech isn’t a conservative issue—at least, it shouldn’t be. It is an AMERICAN value. Everyone benefits from open dialogue, even if you disagree with what people are saying. That’s why I was proud to sign on to a letter to the CEOs of AT&T; and DIRECTV over their [removal of Newsmax](http://links.burchettforcongress.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2f80561774f943d9b514d0549ec6f868&_e=wNR48WDyK11PYFpJqAkW5vQEY5tHSjG3oDepds60TZxdrOIHYA78OaYGZkf-h4zIDn1Kq4gGquO3UH9t_1kS8fxKdK9YhmmdLgX4Jf30a65ALYbXhVeu6kW2fg8bsh8kjAevvVfR4-J8roWeNr8wnOixFOGeXoHx956fY1TPpBXaT8VH6Twi94NYfEtfot_ZpWPkl7F8_svY17MYuAAvCjfU47BM2sO_cVAyub1KLBFJn5KeNKJdXZkIwybc1QL1nWbeIo-H801-58TKZOg6kQ%3D%3D). We need more people like Katrenia in this world. | | | | | Just For Fun 🎉 | | → Neighborly love 😌🌮 () → Valentine’s Day cheer 🥰💐 () → Hypnotic moves 😅😎 () | | | | | Product Of The Month 🛍 | | | | | | | | Please chip in today to help us oust radical Democrats and put a check on the Biden-Harris agenda. | | Stay in Touch 👇 | | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Burchett for Congress

| | | Have you ever had really good seats to a really bad sports event?

Patriot, this is Speaker Kevin McCarthy. I had a front row view of Biden's Socialist Manifesto, and heard nothing but lies, attacks, and even more progressive policy promises...

Now, the liberal media is spinning this speech as a major success for Joe and the Democrats and they will raise MILLIONS as a result today so they can continue running this country into the ground.

So, we have a choice to make. We can give in to Biden's lies and destructive plans, or we can fight for the America we know and love. I know where I stand...

I'm going to fight like _HELL_ as I promised the night I was elected your Speaker of the House. The question is whether you're ready to stand with me.**

That’s the energy state Democrats are counting on as they go toe-to-toe with Republicans in competitive races across the country. name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Democrats can have another House seat if we help Jennifer McClellan win her special election in Virginia in 11 days. She’s doing her part, reaching out to voters. We can do our part, helping her campaign pay for ads, signs, and mailings, making sure this election doesn’t fall through the cracks and give Republicans a pickup opportunity.
body:
Add another House seat this month?
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7f0dd1bd-7e45-4dde-9539-6f95e68aa6e0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, [**We Have Less than Two Weeks To Shrink Kevin McCarthy's Majority — and we can do it if we can help Jennifer McClellan win this special election in Virginia!!**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T98723187-a10b-4a16-987a-e17b24ffdddc/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _In just 11 days, there will be a special election in Virginia's 4th Congressional District. Our incredible Democratic candidate, Jennifer McClellan, would be the first African-American to represent the district and another vote for Leader Hakeem Jeffries!_ She describes herself like this: \--- _I believe in the power of government to make people’s lives better — and I’ve spent the past 18 years as a public servant helping Virginians and solving problems, from passing the Voting Rights Act of Virginia to the Reproductive Health Protection Act.

**But my work doesn’t stop here – because there are still more people to help and problems to solve.**

My team has a plan to win. We’re distributing yard signs, airing new ads, and meeting voters all over the district – but our critical voter outreach efforts are expensive._ _\---_
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc30b1fd6-ee53-46a9-90a7-37b4da16762b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| **McClellan's opponent, Leon Benjamin, is a MAGA NUT who pushed the dangerous conspiracy theory Trump didn't lose the 2020 election and when Benjamin ran for this seat in 2022 and lost, he refused to concede. He's a bigot who attacks immigrants, a** nd _he'll provide Kevin McCarthy another vote in Congress._ **_We must ensure that in 11 days we send Jennifer McClellan, not this nutcase, to Congress. We don't need more Marjorie Taylor Greenes in the Capitol._** [**_Can you join us in rushing Jennifer McClellan $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can so she can put those funds to use and win this crucial congressional seat?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te5b318f2-d94b-4254-ad55-da303abfb506/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Thank you so much, Blue Amp Action community!_ Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta473cc86-3bdf-4e9f-bc93-dbc9d3ac8780/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: CJ Miller, United Rural Democrats
subject: Hey, it’s CJ
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United Rural Democrats](https://click
body:
Peter, it’s nice to meet you. | | | Hi Peter, It's a pleasure to meet you. My name is CJ, and I am an organizer with United Rural Democrats. I've been working with United Rural Democrats for a while now. I've served as a scheduler - helping keep the trains running on time. Now, I'm putting my rural roots and organizing skills to work to make sure that we reach as many rural Americans as possible. I'm from Indiana, and I get involved in my community in as many ways as I can. I even ran a campaign for Monroe County Council at the age of 19 for Peter Iverson. This work is personal to me. **Change happens at the local level, and that's exactly where United Rural Democrats excels.** I'm very fortunate to be working with such a diverse, supportive, and collaborative team. Whether we're taking a road trip to a small rural town to chat with voters, organizing a team meet up to share best practices on connecting with rural Americans, or just sharing our favorite rural traditions, **we embody the spirit of the heartland.** [Will you help our critical work in supporting rural communities by chipping in today?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xNG6r6b6yi4Uz9vCRXD2GcQidgNdlMQkaIXpDZ4YI619F191Wnl15v0vKq7JcfZ4b4QL-WSXQFC1VzKAp1pScyvhtoPUBoKHKNceNM4FugIU4nueMHEn9NQftc6nzY-d2HalBHYJzJQ0J-BQ224n0YeMO_UQr4ZQkCuNaAClPUZkNPVtwoANsG6UnMsISA5RjVHZ0pk3h3DW0oaii8HG7-wrwG8kFHqoJykylSYV4yu2UKq3Me2LBkOQgBQfH7rt_AkAluBW72BEH911LQZ7dy/3tl/CyRpW-66Ti25KfWRjggheg/h0/8oK7kt8S6PLP4VPOHoD5B6WIhsceD-T0AdgfgeM8-DQ) **[CHIP IN $5 HERE](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xNG6r6b6yi4Uz9vCRXD2Gc0zZXQuP5iaFLRM2K0eGWMr8RL2eYUjID8QYOr0Kc-4Vib4Uu7hE_D_Och4M4ZimATboJ1I9izdId5ztq8Q38wgCzJBpkoDpmQwWJo067yU7kjOVIOdPnDPmTRinGoav45pIZJncBSuxKgk-j8cCwVIIo_HbyzUTctUYDFvqNGyOocJj8MSSCaxNiHiPfk8Z1a3dB-EAwFX-hG8Q39uVIX4zxiag1Dd-rBSMdwezzdQWAWVLgDLoD9ouDOaKpO8XNiNAPw0nCOOsrwTj-r6QiIA/3tl/CyRpW-66Ti25KfWRjggheg/h1/P39Au_ORur5YqRr7n5eczqZ-uWCmdd5r0-xn1vLFoAg)** **[CONTRIBUTE A RECURRING DONATION](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xNG6r6b6yi4Uz9vCRXD2Gchfmt0Un8hoUB8ntnqemubhlImmZRs7_MQVTN_YCMYP-Z2c_2zpg3DRIDeUP7glnTQy5FHIhYo8eAlYAPJsgoLZWvoprv422KaoYds2fT7iPpTHys61G6jpGl0DdYyayLVyYsXgrBlTsTSRz-4wybBebJ1GvHocHMz6-LSA7J50-pZZYYW6J4w2JAb45TDKAPtUY4wxMwtpUorunC03EM-hUcAbJvwTC24Ri1Jw1slSGIB3CP1kPwQz1jAaxApJgB64cdCIfukE-6of88FwaalQ/3tl/CyRpW-66Ti25KfWRjggheg/h2/aaSKpCB974Jm-Iso7g0IMip3b8g-iuY3pThbL08UCIk)** Thank you for being on this team with me, and I look forward to working to continue URD's mission and promote progress in rural America. – CJ Miller
United Rural Democrats

If we're going to take back our country, we need a 50-state strategy to organize in every community across the country. **Will you support our network of activists on the ground as we work to rebuild our party's infrastructure and flip these areas blue?**

I've been working with United Rural Democrats for a while now. I've served as a scheduler - helping keep the trains running on time. Now, I'm putting my rural roots and organizing skills to work to make sure that we reach as many rural Americans as possible. I'm from Indiana, and I get involved in my community in as many ways as I can. I even ran a campaign for Monroe County Council at the age of 19 for Peter Iverson. This work is personal to me. Change happens at the local level, and that's exactly where United Rural Democrats excels. I'm very fortunate to be working with such a diverse, supportive, and collaborative team. Whether we're taking a road trip to a small rural town to chat with voters, organizing a team meet up to share best practices on connecting with rural Americans, or just sharing our favorite rural traditions, we embody the spirit of the heartland. Thank you for being on this team with me, and I look forward to working to continue URD's mission and promote progress in rural America. – CJ Miller United Rural Democrats United Rural Democrats (URD) is leading the effort to rebuild the Democratic Party in rural America. If we're going to take back our country, we need a 50-state strategy to organize in every community across the country. Will you support our network of activists on the ground as we work to rebuild our party's infrastructure and flip these areas blue?

name: Charlie Kirk, Turning Point USA

Fellow Patriot, I am EXPOSING the College Cartel for the first time. _And now I want to send YOU a copy of the evidence against America's colleges and universities -- my NEW BOOK,_**The College Scam.** [**Will you claim YOUR copy of _The College Scam_ right now? All I'm asking for in exchange is a gift of ANY AMOUNT, even just a dollar, to Turning Point USA.**](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=f8d10d1ebdda45da8bbfe4d9fb0067fa&_e=iI-My8rpbKW5HGKDLs1v9jxiXafqNZ3yGJL7H42TaIf_-sdq-Vsmi9QkDfhp0Fo5R9RPdT27nnann6j3xZiBzP_TiOtlXK6GhvmqCcXSwd8dDjQBfqqbLUTJU2V1o1pPHf83hhHE2xSloFTpNA1LX07CcOHBSuImJ_6Iz4jTYUjVtrlOzqnvcPP15p3IssiS1VfLYOB-13DXJSECho2NIMcVWWLllakuJYgCDe5EURKNN4tFFUqPadXa1YHZDl7-4dP-yyzqLU3depUtuLnqywiwjdXDoOyo1BbkfG8GVyQ%3D)
